Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14412:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7049:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5922:   153	- (Q4.a) Spot-check: re-run `cargo test --workspace`; expect 265 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14413:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7097:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7471:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14414:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7261:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7635:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5109:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14415:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7262:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7636:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14417:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7275:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7649:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5508:    48	- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14427:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7393:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7767:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8129:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14428:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7397:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7771:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8145:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14430:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7400:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7774:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8152:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:48:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14445:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7993:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8724:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6381:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:19:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14450:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8185:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8950:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14464:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8904:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7148:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7660:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14465:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8905:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:7149:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:7661:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14468:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8947:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9341:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14469:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8953:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9709:    48	- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14496:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9451:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R3.md:5568:    48	- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14515:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12860:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:22:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14566:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5437:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14567:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5438:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14569:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5812:    43	| `cargo test --workspace` PASS | **265** | re-runnable; all suites green |
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14572:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5815:    46	| `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` | **16/16 PASS** | proxy routing + round-robin conformance (also wrapped by `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so it runs every `cargo test --workspace`) |
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14574:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5922:   153	- (Q4.a) Spot-check: re-run `cargo test --workspace`; expect 265 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14576:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7471:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14589:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7766:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8128:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14590:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7767:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8129:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14608:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8724:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6381:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:19:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14609:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8794:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:8200:- cargo test --workspace: 265 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14610:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8798:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:8291:- cargo test --workspace: 265 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14612:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8941:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:153:- (Q4.a) Spot-check: re-run `cargo test --workspace`; expect 265 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14613:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8950:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14614:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9113:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5108:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14628:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9916:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9341:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14629:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9917:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9342:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14630:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9933:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9709:    48	- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14647:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:12561:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:21:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:92:    **Finding**: PASS (Fully Addressed). Yes. My previous challenge noted that coupling redemption to specific transaction types via `ResolutionRef` was brittle. By removing `ResolutionRef` and having the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` check the canonical `task_markets_t.state` enum directly, the design is now much more extensible and robust. If a future TB introduces a new resolution mechanism, it only needs to mutate `task_markets_t.state` to a resolved value; the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` logic will not need to be changed. This is a significant architectural improvement that makes future evolution easier.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:103:-   **Recommendation**: **PROCEED to SHIP**
handover/audits/GEMINI_STAGE_C_OVERALL_AUDIT_2026-05-09_R1.md:41:Q9: PASS - The user's multi-clause authorization from session #32 is structurally equivalent to a Class-4 §8 sign-off. The §8 packet's §6 analysis is correct: the first clause contains the key acts of `授权` (authorization) and `自主执行` (autonomous execution) for a defined scope (`直到polymarket全部落地`). This aligns with the requirements in `CLAUDE.md` §10. The packet correctly frames this forward-grant as being conditional on the successful PASS of the PRE-§8 dual audit process, which this audit is part of.

handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:18:    *   **Verification**: Spec § 5.2.5 defines the canonical `ChallengeWindow::is_open(now)` helper. § 3.2 `challenge_transition` and § 3.4 `finalize_reward_transition` both contain explicit comments and pseudocode invoking this helper. This eliminates the risk of logic drift between the two critical state transitions that guard the challenge window boundary.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:21:    *   **Verification**: Spec § 5.2.6 provides explicit prose confirming that the sequencer's atomic `next_logical_t()` assignment is the canonical tie-breaker for concurrent submissions. This is a standard, robust, and deterministic solution for establishing a total order on transactions, which is sufficient to prevent state divergence under concurrent submission.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:28:The v1.4 patches did **NOT** introduce any new issues. The changes were, as claimed, cosmetic and clarifying. They either corrected documentation (§ 5.3), refactored pseudocode to use a canonical helper (§ 3.2, § 3.4), or added explicit prose to describe existing deterministic mechanisms (§ 5.2.6, § 2.5). These changes reduce ambiguity and risk; they do not add any new logic or surface area for bugs.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:48:*   **Drift Detection**: Drift between branch A and branch B on the bincode rule can be detected mechanically from the v1.4 spec. The rule defined in § 2.5 is precise enough that a single, simple transaction object can be used as a reference. If branch A and branch B produce different byte-level serializations (and thus different `canonical_digest()` outputs) for this reference object, drift is proven.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:49:*   **Role of Fixtures**: The full fixture corpus is for *conformance testing* (i.e., ensuring an implementation correctly handles a wide range of edge cases), not for *defining the canonical format*. The spec itself defines the format. Therefore, the fixtures are not load-bearing for the STEP_B comparison metric of "do both branches produce compatible, signed objects?". The spec alone is sufficient for that.
handover/audits/GEMINI_INV8_DAG_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:19:**PASS.** Yes. This ambiguity is resolved before the DAG algorithm is ever invoked. The `TransitionLedger` input is a snapshot of L4 state. Per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1.4` § 5.2.1 and § 5.2.6, the L4 sequencer assigns a monotonic, atomic `logical_t` to every transaction, creating a canonical total ordering. For any given resource read by a transaction, the `producer_tx` is therefore a single, unambiguously defined transaction in the ledger's history. The DAG algorithm correctly consumes this pre-resolved state.
handover/audits/GEMINI_INV8_DAG_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:28:**PASS.** Yes. Self-reference is implicitly handled by the BFS `visited` set (see H6). Adversarial cycles in the ledger (which would imply a corrupt state) are explicitly detected by the `assert_acyclic` function in Step 5, which correctly uses a topological sort based on the canonical `(timestamp_logical, tx_id)` key. A panic is the appropriate response to discovering a fundamentally broken ledger state.
handover/evidence/tb_6_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-01/README.md:20:- The synthetic `TaskOpenTx` canonical encoding (referenced by `tx_payload_cid` in the L4 entry's `entry_canonical` blob).
handover/evidence/tb_6_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-01/README.md:21:- The `LedgerEntry` canonical encoding for the accepted TaskOpen (referenced by the git tree blob `entry_canonical` at commit `38f7112f`).
handover/evidence/tb_6_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-01/README.md:30:- Tree: `payload_cid` (CID of synthetic `TaskOpenTx`) + `signature` (system Ed25519) + `entry_canonical` (260-byte canonical encoding of `LedgerEntry`)
handover/evidence/tb_6_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-01/README.md:87:- The accepted TaskOpen's `tx_payload_cid` → CAS lookup → `canonical_decode` → reconstructs `TypedTx::TaskOpen(TaskOpenTx { ... })`.
handover/evidence/tb_6_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-01/README.md:161:`gp_payload="nlinarith"` matches the canonical OMEGA proof for this theorem (TB-0 baseline + TB-1 Day-1 + TB-2..TB-5 ship). Capability replicates under chaintape mode.

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:1201:| FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.7 | `audit_tape` binary view-shares / view-pools / view-prices commands MUST resolve their data through ref-derived state, not through any in-memory shortcut. |
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:1211:| CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 | NO change to canonical signing payload. C2 changes ledger storage form, not signed contents. |

(3) Removed per-append `kernel.create_market(node_id, system_lp_amount)` call. (4) `halt_and_settle` no longer calls `kernel.resolve_all` (deleted); fires RunEnd + tool.on_halt only. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental hash 346a6a3c superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental (2026-05-05; preseed-settle barrier added BEFORE the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS iter loop): r2 smoke surfaced a race that the per-iter Codex CHALLENGE #2 fix (removing pre-iter await) inadvertently exposed. The preseed phase queues TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED seed via bus.submit_typed_tx but does NOT block on their commits. Without a settle barrier, iter=0's q_snapshot can fall in the middle of the preseed queue → iter-0 WorkTx parent_state_root captured before another preseed tx commits → apply_one rejects iter-0 with StaleParent → iter-0 lands on L4.E → iter-1's snap.price_index empty → v2_pick=None for iter-1 (the r2 smoke shape: tx_kind_counts.work=3 / l4e_count=2 / iter-1 v2_pick=None instead of expected Some(iter-0)). Fix: poll q_snapshot at start of FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS block until state_root is stable for one poll cycle (preseed queue drained); 50 × 200ms = 10s budget; FAIL-CLOSED if barrier doesn't settle (signals wedged queue). This matches the "settle then dispatch" pattern that the original (commit b5118fd) hook accidentally got via the per-iter pre-await — the per-iter pre-await was structurally on the wrong side of submit (Codex CH2) but it ALSO accidentally drained the preseed queue before iter 0's parent_state_root capture. The settle barrier is the principled replacement. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (without barrier) hash fada36b4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual-audit closure of Codex VETO #1..#4 + CHALLENGE #1..#4 + Gemini Q1/Q2/Q5/Q7/Q8/Q12 CHALLENGES on parent commit b5118fd): (V2 Codex VETO #2 — produced_worktx_ids.push moved to AFTER tb8_await_state_root_advance success; bus.submit_typed_tx is async per src/bus.rs:136-138, so push-before-commit could register a queued tx_id that subsequently failed dispatch). (V4 Codex VETO #4 — env-var parse + CAS / ProposalTelemetry / WorkTx-construction / submit / commit-await failures all converted from warn-and-skip to FAIL-CLOSED via std::process::exit(3); for an explicit user-activated arena hook, partial-success silently biasing the entropy distribution is unacceptable). (CH2 Codex CHALLENGE #2 — pre-iteration tb8_await_state_root_advance was placed BEFORE the tx it was supposed to observe (helper contract per src/runtime/adapter.rs:570-576 says "submit, then await POST-submit"); fix removes the pre-await and only retains the post-submit await per iteration so the next iter's snapshot reflects the prior commit). (CH4 Codex CHALLENGE #4 — removed produced_worktx_ids.last() fallback when v2_pick is None; strict semantics now: parent_tx tracks the v2 selector's authoritative pick or None for root, never a manufactured edge from local state). (V4 supplemental — sequencer.q_snapshot Err converted to FAIL-CLOSED exit). Smoke script `run_tb_16_x_2_4_smoke_2026-05-05.sh` companion fixes (V3): set -euo pipefail (was -uo); explicit RC capture via `|| RC=$?` + abort on RC ≠ 0; refuse to overwrite populated PROBLEM_DIR without ALLOW_REUSE=1; audit_tape stdout captured into AT_LOG variable so pipefail propagates the binary RC. .2.3 + .2.5 smoke scripts also receive the set -e + RC capture carry-over (cheap consistency fix). BOLTZMANN_EPSILON_NUM/DEN env vars (default 9/10) injected so the v2 selector's epsilon-greedy uniform-random branch fires 90% of the time on iter 1+ (vs 10% default), producing diverse parent_tx instead of always-iter-0 lex-tiebreak. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4 hash 5a989d15 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.4 — Multi-WorkTx + Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS=<staker>:<count>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional .2.5 bankruptcy-after-accepted seed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects N (≥3) real-signed WorkTxs serially. Each iteration: (1) snapshot bus → call boltzmann_select_parent_v2(snap.price_index, snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut seeded_rng) — the existing TB-14 Atom 5 integer-rational scheduler (sdk/actor.rs:57); (2) parent_tx = v2 pick OR fallback to most recent produced WorkTx tx_id (so iter 1+ has SOME parent; iter 0 is genuinely root); (3) build + write ProposalTelemetry to CAS via build_for_evaluator_append_with_parent(parent_tx) (mandatory for audit assertion id=24 chain integrity per .2.5 r2 lesson); (4) construct real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) referencing the telemetry CID as proposal_cid; (5) submit + await commit; (6) capture tx_id for next iteration's fallback. Closes the missing R3 "Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise" gap: prior chains had at most 1 admitted WorkTx per task, so the v2 selector had no candidate set to choose from. Audit assertion id=43 (boltzmann_parent_selection_diversity, Layer E supplemental, added in .2.5 commit) verifies Shannon entropy ≥ 0.25 across same-task ProposalTelemetry.parent_tx distribution. DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.4 said "verify boltzmann_select_parent_v2 is called in WorkTx admission path; STEP_B-PROTOCOL TRIGGERED". Position taken: STEP_B refers to sequencer-side dispatch path; v2 selector is ALREADY called proposal-side at evaluator.rs:1828 (captured into _v2_canonical_pick, currently unused). For SG-16.x.2.4 (≥3 WorkTx with parent_selection_entropy ≥ 0.5), the parent_tx record lives in ProposalTelemetry (CAS object) — proposal-time data, not sequencer-side admission data. No sequencer.rs touch needed; STEP_B not triggered. Class 3 dual external audit (Codex + Gemini) STILL applied because the surface is high-impact (Boltzmann RUNTIME wire-up is the V3L-14 anti-star-topology mechanism; Art II.2.1 alarm threshold gate). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r3 hash d39c67d1 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 r3 (2026-05-05; smoke r2 surfaced audit assertion id=24 (proposal_telemetry_chain) HALT — the seed WorkTx's proposal_cid was computed via Cid::from_content of a literal string but the bytes were never written to CAS, so cas.get(proposal_cid) returned NotFound, halting the audit. Fix: the seed-WorkTx hook now opens CasStore::open(&bundle.cas_path), constructs a minimal ProposalTelemetry via build_for_evaluator_append (which writes proposal_artifact bytes to CAS as a side-effect), then calls proposal_telemetry::write_to_cas to persist the telemetry itself and uses the returned CID as the WorkTx.proposal_cid. ProposalTelemetry parent_tx is None for the .2.5 seed (single-WorkTx scenario; .2.4 will use Boltzmann pick). verification_result_cid is None (no Lean verification on the seed; id=24 only halts on missing telemetry CAS bytes, NOT on missing VerificationResult). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r2 hash c19dfcbe (which broke audit id=24) superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.5 — AutopsyCapsule real-bankruptcy chain): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED=<staker>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects a real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) so stakes_t has ≥1 entry for `task-{run_id}` at the time TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 emits TaskBankruptcyTx (line ~3150). The TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5 hook (sequencer.rs:1374) then calls derive_autopsies_for_bankruptcy(pre_econ, bk, ...) which iterates stakes_t and emits an AgentAutopsyCapsule with loss_reason_class=Bankruptcy + loss_amount = stake.amount. Closes the missing R2 P4 path "WorkTx-accepted → got accepted → then the task went bankrupt". The seeded WorkTx is REAL (real Ed25519 signature via AgentKeypairRegistry, real predicate_results, real stake debit from preseeded balance) — NOT a synthetic-rejection gate (compare make_synthetic_worktx at line ~1086 which uses synthetic_rejection_for_l4e_gate=true → L4.E only). Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 to materialize WorkTx (admit) → TerminalSummary → TaskBankruptcy → AutopsyCapsule in CAS + AutopsyIndex entry in agent_autopsies_t. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.3 hash e1c4d057 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.3 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.3 — CompleteSetRedeem env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_REDEEM=<owner>:<outcome>:<share_units> env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (parallel to FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE; OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional so it fires on both OMEGA-Confirm success and MaxTxExhausted+FORCE_BANKRUPTCY paths), constructs an agent-signed CompleteSetRedeemTx via make_real_complete_set_redeem_signed_by + submits via bus.submit_typed_tx. Pairs with TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED (mints provider's YES+NO shares) + TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY (transitions market to Bankrupt → NO wins) to produce a single chain containing CompleteSetMint → TaskBankruptcy → CompleteSetRedeem sequence — closes the missing 6th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 11-of-13 → 12-of-13). DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.3 spec'd 4 env-var parts (owner:event_id:outcome:share); implementation uses 3 (owner:outcome:share_units) — event_id auto-derived from `task-{run_id}` because run_id contains a unix-ms timestamp minted at evaluator entry (run_id.rs:21) and is unpredictable from the smoke script. Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's auto-derive pattern (line ~3154). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix hash 12489ab4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix (2026-05-05; FC1-emit hook placement bug-fix on TB-16.x.2.2 commit 5e32cbf): the original 5e32cbf placed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE in the post-bundle cleanup section reachable ONLY on the MaxTxExhausted exit path (line 2895 P0-A comment + 2902 mark_final_accept). FORCE_CHALLENGER fires on the OMEGA-Confirm success path which early-returns at make_pput before that cleanup. This fix mirrors the FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE block onto BOTH OMEGA-Confirm exit paths (full-proof @ ~line 2253 + per-tactic @ ~line 2755), each with post-emit tb8_await_state_root_advance because the OMEGA exit drops bundle without bundle.shutdown drain (evaluator.rs:2936-2938). Original commit's claim "two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive" remains accurate — but the OMEGA path had NO cleanup section at all. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash 993452e4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. Predecessor TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up hash 3530a2d5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up (2026-05-03; architect ruling step 1 — Codex R1 primary VETO closure): the canonical TxId returned by `boltzmann_select_parent_v2` MUST NOT flow into legacy shadow `bus.append` parent_id (kernel.tape uses a different (shadow) id namespace; canonical TxId becomes a dangling citation → kernel.append rejects). Per architect ruling: "Use None unless a real shadow id exists." No canonical → shadow id mapping is currently available; pass None (legacy default). The v2 selector still runs (its result captured as `_v2_canonical_pick` for observability + future canonical wire-up — see B′ step 4 CanonicalNodeGraph + parent_tx replacement for last_tx_by_agent), but its output is explicitly NOT passed to bus.append. Closes Codex R1 primary VETO defect #1.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 (2026-05-03; closing OBS_TB_12_LEGACY_CPMM_QUARANTINE): production wire-swap. (1) Imports updated: `BoltzmannParams + boltzmann_select_parent` (legacy f64) → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2 + BoltzmannMaskPolicy` (integer-rational). (2) `BusConfig` literals (×2) drop `system_lp_amount` field. (3) `let params = BoltzmannParams::from_env()` → `let policy = BoltzmannMaskPolicy::from_env()`. (4) Tick-time logging derives `market_count` + top-N price ticker from `bus.snapshot().price_index` (cross-multiplication argmax; rendered `n/d`, never decimal). (5) Per-tx prompt build derives `market_ticker_str` from `snap.price_index` + queries `prompt_balance` from `bus.sequencer.q_snapshot().economic_state_t.balances_t` (replaces legacy `snap.get_balance`; the `f64` is the `prompt.rs` render contract — `prompt.rs` is not a TB-14 module surface). (6) Boltzmann selector call replaced: `boltzmann_select_parent(&snap.tape, &prices, &params, &mut rng)` → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2(&snap.price_index, &snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut rng).map(|tx| tx.0)`. Mirror of TB-8 tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify pattern. Predecessor TB-10 Atom 1+3 hash a5b30cd1 superseded.

Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E hash 3e770c4b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E (2026-05-05; tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible eligibility-boundary semantic-fix): the original TB-16.x.2.2 helper used `if elapsed <= window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` — i.e. require elapsed > delta. With delta=0 the helper required ≥1 round to pass, contradicting its own docstring claim "delta=0 makes every Open case immediately eligible". Smoke r3 (post-Patch-A) showed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE batch returning count=0 because q.q_t.current_round is NOT auto-advanced per-tx (unlike tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible's caller-supplied current_logical_t which auto-advances per-tx); the OMEGA-Confirm path opens the case and scans within the same round, so elapsed=0 for delta=0 was always skipped. Patch E flips the boundary to `if elapsed < window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` (require elapsed >= delta), aligning impl with docstring. Sole callers of this helper today are the FORCE_-prefixed arena hooks; no production caller depends on the prior off-by-one boundary. Docstring updated to reflect `>= delta` and to clarify the time-unit difference vs tb11. handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6088:273:"src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs" = "723dadf6dc824b2de37be55cf599d2fedf75ff4ea588be51b549e5eb2d71aff3"  # rehashed by Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 R3 (2026-05-08; charter STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2 / SG-A3.3): CasStore::put now invokes Git2LedgerWriter::advance_chaintape_cas_to(repo_path, git_oid, ...) after durable sidecar append, advancing refs/chaintape/cas to track each new CAS blob OID. Best-effort (`let _ = ...`) so a transient ref-update failure does NOT roll back the durable CAS write — the ref is overwrite-safe (force=true) and recovers on the next put. Pure additive; no change to put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). Rules: min_liquidity > 0 (non-positive → default); price_margin numerator+denominator both > 0 (zero either → default); beta_den > 0 (zero → default); beta_num >= 0 (negative → default); epsilon ∈ [0, 1] (den > 0 AND num ≤ den; otherwise → default pair). Fail-soft per Art.I.1 + C-027 — invalid env value falls back to that field's Default; misconfiguration must not crash the swarm but production semantics must remain inside the architect-mandated invariant envelope. +11 inline tests pinning each rule (negative/zero boundary checks per field; epsilon ≥ 0 and ≤ 1 boundaries explicitly accepted).
