Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 hash 5dfd5142 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual external audit R2 closure): Codex R2 OVERALL CHALLENGE/ship-clean (all 4 R1 VETOs closed; only doc-drift residual). Gemini R2 OVERALL VETO on Q1 (architectural enforcement gap) + Q2 (degenerate smoke). Conservative resolution per feedback_dual_audit_conflict (VETO>CHALLENGE>PASS): file OBS_R024 + TB-17 PRE-17.5 prerequisite (sequencer-side Boltzmann enforcement gate, Class 4 surface, deferred per umbrella charter §0 Class 3 envelope respect + project_tb11_to_tb17_roadmap phase boundary). Q2 closed in r4 (BOLTZMANN_SEED=12345 → distribution {iter-0:1, iter-1:2} non-trivial). This commit's source change: stale-comment cleanup ONLY (no functional code change) — evaluator.rs:1241-1250 docblock above the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS env-var check now mentions "no fallback (since .fix r1)" and "non-None Shannon entropy ≥ 0.5" instead of the pre-fix-r1 wording about fallback to produced_worktx_ids.last() and 0.25 threshold. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental hash 346a6a3c superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental (2026-05-05; preseed-settle barrier added BEFORE the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS iter loop): r2 smoke surfaced a race that the per-iter Codex CHALLENGE #2 fix (removing pre-iter await) inadvertently exposed. The preseed phase queues TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED seed via bus.submit_typed_tx but does NOT block on their commits. Without a settle barrier, iter=0's q_snapshot can fall in the middle of the preseed queue → iter-0 WorkTx parent_state_root captured before another preseed tx commits → apply_one rejects iter-0 with StaleParent → iter-0 lands on L4.E → iter-1's snap.price_index empty → v2_pick=None for iter-1 (the r2 smoke shape: tx_kind_counts.work=3 / l4e_count=2 / iter-1 v2_pick=None instead of expected Some(iter-0)). Fix: poll q_snapshot at start of FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS block until state_root is stable for one poll cycle (preseed queue drained); 50 × 200ms = 10s budget; FAIL-CLOSED if barrier doesn't settle (signals wedged queue). This matches the "settle then dispatch" pattern that the original (commit b5118fd) hook accidentally got via the per-iter pre-await — the per-iter pre-await was structurally on the wrong side of submit (Codex CH2) but it ALSO accidentally drained the preseed queue before iter 0's parent_state_root capture. The settle barrier is the principled replacement. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (without barrier) hash fada36b4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual-audit closure of Codex VETO #1..#4 + CHALLENGE #1..#4 + Gemini Q1/Q2/Q5/Q7/Q8/Q12 CHALLENGES on parent commit b5118fd): (V2 Codex VETO #2 — produced_worktx_ids.push moved to AFTER tb8_await_state_root_advance success; bus.submit_typed_tx is async per src/bus.rs:136-138, so push-before-commit could register a queued tx_id that subsequently failed dispatch). (V4 Codex VETO #4 — env-var parse + CAS / ProposalTelemetry / WorkTx-construction / submit / commit-await failures all converted from warn-and-skip to FAIL-CLOSED via std::process::exit(3); for an explicit user-activated arena hook, partial-success silently biasing the entropy distribution is unacceptable). (CH2 Codex CHALLENGE #2 — pre-iteration tb8_await_state_root_advance was placed BEFORE the tx it was supposed to observe (helper contract per src/runtime/adapter.rs:570-576 says "submit, then await POST-submit"); fix removes the pre-await and only retains the post-submit await per iteration so the next iter's snapshot reflects the prior commit). (CH4 Codex CHALLENGE #4 — removed produced_worktx_ids.last() fallback when v2_pick is None; strict semantics now: parent_tx tracks the v2 selector's authoritative pick or None for root, never a manufactured edge from local state). (V4 supplemental — sequencer.q_snapshot Err converted to FAIL-CLOSED exit). Smoke script `run_tb_16_x_2_4_smoke_2026-05-05.sh` companion fixes (V3): set -euo pipefail (was -uo); explicit RC capture via `|| RC=$?` + abort on RC ≠ 0; refuse to overwrite populated PROBLEM_DIR without ALLOW_REUSE=1; audit_tape stdout captured into AT_LOG variable so pipefail propagates the binary RC. .2.3 + .2.5 smoke scripts also receive the set -e + RC capture carry-over (cheap consistency fix). BOLTZMANN_EPSILON_NUM/DEN env vars (default 9/10) injected so the v2 selector's epsilon-greedy uniform-random branch fires 90% of the time on iter 1+ (vs 10% default), producing diverse parent_tx instead of always-iter-0 lex-tiebreak. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4 hash 5a989d15 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.4 — Multi-WorkTx + Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS=<staker>:<count>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional .2.5 bankruptcy-after-accepted seed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects N (≥3) real-signed WorkTxs serially. Each iteration: (1) snapshot bus → call boltzmann_select_parent_v2(snap.price_index, snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut seeded_rng) — the existing TB-14 Atom 5 integer-rational scheduler (sdk/actor.rs:57); (2) parent_tx = v2 pick OR fallback to most recent produced WorkTx tx_id (so iter 1+ has SOME parent; iter 0 is genuinely root); (3) build + write ProposalTelemetry to CAS via build_for_evaluator_append_with_parent(parent_tx) (mandatory for audit assertion id=24 chain integrity per .2.5 r2 lesson); (4) construct real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) referencing the telemetry CID as proposal_cid; (5) submit + await commit; (6) capture tx_id for next iteration's fallback. Closes the missing R3 "Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise" gap: prior chains had at most 1 admitted WorkTx per task, so the v2 selector had no candidate set to choose from. Audit assertion id=43 (boltzmann_parent_selection_diversity, Layer E supplemental, added in .2.5 commit) verifies Shannon entropy ≥ 0.25 across same-task ProposalTelemetry.parent_tx distribution. DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.4 said "verify boltzmann_select_parent_v2 is called in WorkTx admission path; STEP_B-PROTOCOL TRIGGERED". Position taken: STEP_B refers to sequencer-side dispatch path; v2 selector is ALREADY called proposal-side at evaluator.rs:1828 (captured into _v2_canonical_pick, currently unused). For SG-16.x.2.4 (≥3 WorkTx with parent_selection_entropy ≥ 0.5), the parent_tx record lives in ProposalTelemetry (CAS object) — proposal-time data, not sequencer-side admission data. No sequencer.rs touch needed; STEP_B not triggered. Class 3 dual external audit (Codex + Gemini) STILL applied because the surface is high-impact (Boltzmann RUNTIME wire-up is the V3L-14 anti-star-topology mechanism; Art II.2.1 alarm threshold gate). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r3 hash d39c67d1 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 r3 (2026-05-05; smoke r2 surfaced audit assertion id=24 (proposal_telemetry_chain) HALT — the seed WorkTx's proposal_cid was computed via Cid::from_content of a literal string but the bytes were never written to CAS, so cas.get(proposal_cid) returned NotFound, halting the audit. Fix: the seed-WorkTx hook now opens CasStore::open(&bundle.cas_path), constructs a minimal ProposalTelemetry via build_for_evaluator_append (which writes proposal_artifact bytes to CAS as a side-effect), then calls proposal_telemetry::write_to_cas to persist the telemetry itself and uses the returned CID as the WorkTx.proposal_cid. ProposalTelemetry parent_tx is None for the .2.5 seed (single-WorkTx scenario; .2.4 will use Boltzmann pick). verification_result_cid is None (no Lean verification on the seed; id=24 only halts on missing telemetry CAS bytes, NOT on missing VerificationResult). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r2 hash c19dfcbe (which broke audit id=24) superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.5 — AutopsyCapsule real-bankruptcy chain): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED=<staker>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects a real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) so stakes_t has ≥1 entry for `task-{run_id}` at the time TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 emits TaskBankruptcyTx (line ~3150). The TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5 hook (sequencer.rs:1374) then calls derive_autopsies_for_bankruptcy(pre_econ, bk, ...) which iterates stakes_t and emits an AgentAutopsyCapsule with loss_reason_class=Bankruptcy + loss_amount = stake.amount. Closes the missing R2 P4 path "WorkTx-accepted → got accepted → then the task went bankrupt". The seeded WorkTx is REAL (real Ed25519 signature via AgentKeypairRegistry, real predicate_results, real stake debit from preseeded balance) — NOT a synthetic-rejection gate (compare make_synthetic_worktx at line ~1086 which uses synthetic_rejection_for_l4e_gate=true → L4.E only). Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 to materialize WorkTx (admit) → TerminalSummary → TaskBankruptcy → AutopsyCapsule in CAS + AutopsyIndex entry in agent_autopsies_t. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.3 hash e1c4d057 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.3 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.3 — CompleteSetRedeem env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_REDEEM=<owner>:<outcome>:<share_units> env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (parallel to FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE; OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional so it fires on both OMEGA-Confirm success and MaxTxExhausted+FORCE_BANKRUPTCY paths), constructs an agent-signed CompleteSetRedeemTx via make_real_complete_set_redeem_signed_by + submits via bus.submit_typed_tx. Pairs with TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED (mints provider's YES+NO shares) + TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY (transitions market to Bankrupt → NO wins) to produce a single chain containing CompleteSetMint → TaskBankruptcy → CompleteSetRedeem sequence — closes the missing 6th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 11-of-13 → 12-of-13). DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.3 spec'd 4 env-var parts (owner:event_id:outcome:share); implementation uses 3 (owner:outcome:share_units) — event_id auto-derived from `task-{run_id}` because run_id contains a unix-ms timestamp minted at evaluator entry (run_id.rs:21) and is unpredictable from the smoke script. Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's auto-derive pattern (line ~3154). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix hash 12489ab4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix (2026-05-05; FC1-emit hook placement bug-fix on TB-16.x.2.2 commit 5e32cbf): the original 5e32cbf placed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE in the post-bundle cleanup section reachable ONLY on the MaxTxExhausted exit path (line 2895 P0-A comment + 2902 mark_final_accept). FORCE_CHALLENGER fires on the OMEGA-Confirm success path which early-returns at make_pput before that cleanup. As filed, smoke r2 produced tx_kind_counts.challenge_resolve=0 + id=42 SKIPPED + ship-gate false-positive (grep matched the field name "challenge_resolve" with value 0 + dashboard run_id slug). This fix mirrors the FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE block onto BOTH OMEGA-Confirm exit paths (full-proof @ ~line 2253 + per-tactic @ ~line 2755), each with post-emit tb8_await_state_root_advance because the OMEGA exit drops bundle without bundle.shutdown drain (evaluator.rs:2936-2938). Original commit's claim "two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive" remains accurate — but the OMEGA path had NO cleanup section at all. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash 993452e4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE=1 env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (deliberately OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional, since FORCE_CHALLENGER fires only on the OMEGA-Confirm success path and the two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive), calls tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible(seq, window_delta_logical_t=0, default_resolution=Released) so every Open ChallengeCase is immediately eligible. Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER on the same arena profile to produce a single chain containing Challenge → ChallengeResolve parent-child relationship — closes the missing 5th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 10-of-13 → 11-of-13). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.1 hash eb718a23 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.1 (2026-05-04; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.1 — TaskExpire env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_EXPIRE=1 env-var: after MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule write + (optional) FORCE_BANKRUPTCY emit, calls tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible(seq, current_logical_t=next_logical_t_peek(), expiry_delta_logical_t=0) so every Open/Bankrupt task with elapsed > 0 emits a TaskExpireTx (ExpireReason::Deadline for Open; ExpireReason::BankruptcyTriggered when FORCE_BANKRUPTCY also fired). Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's tb8_await_state_root_advance(pre_ex_root, 5000) prelude so prior commits land before the q_snapshot read. Closes the missing 4th system-emitted tx kind in R3 Round 2 chain (raises 9-of-13 → 10-of-13). Predecessor TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 hash 729a4c5e superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 (2026-05-04; architect §7.3 FR-16.3 + FR-16.4 + FR-16.7): ADDITIVE arena driver hooks. (1) TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER=<agent_id> env-var: after VerifyTx OMEGA-Confirm and before FinalizeReward emit, the named agent submits a real-signed ChallengeTx targeting the WorkTx (FR-16.3). (2) TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED=<provider>:<amount_micro> env-var: after preseed EscrowLock, the named provider submits MarketSeedTx + CompleteSetMintTx against the task's EventId (FR-16.4). (3) TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 env-var: after MAX_TX exhaustion + TerminalSummary emit, sequencer.emit_system_tx(TaskBankruptcy{...}) fires referencing the same EvidenceCapsule (FR-16.7 → autopsy emission via TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5). All three are env-var-gated; no behavior change to existing user-task / preseed paths. Predecessor TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up hash 3530a2d5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up (2026-05-03; architect ruling step 1 — Codex R1 primary VETO closure): the canonical TxId returned by `boltzmann_select_parent_v2` MUST NOT flow into legacy shadow `bus.append` parent_id (kernel.tape uses a different (shadow) id namespace; canonical TxId becomes a dangling citation → kernel.append rejects). Per architect ruling: "Use None unless a real shadow id exists." No canonical → shadow id mapping is currently available; pass None (legacy default). The v2 selector still runs (its result captured as `_v2_canonical_pick` for observability + future canonical wire-up — see B′ step 4 CanonicalNodeGraph + parent_tx replacement for last_tx_by_agent), but its output is explicitly NOT passed to bus.append. Closes Codex R1 primary VETO defect #1.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 (2026-05-03; closing OBS_TB_12_LEGACY_CPMM_QUARANTINE): production wire-swap. (1) Imports updated: `BoltzmannParams + boltzmann_select_parent` (legacy f64) → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2 + BoltzmannMaskPolicy` (integer-rational). (2) `BusConfig` literals (×2) drop `system_lp_amount` field. (3) `let params = BoltzmannParams::from_env()` → `let policy = BoltzmannMaskPolicy::from_env()`. (4) Tick-time logging derives `market_count` + top-N price ticker from `bus.snapshot().price_index` (cross-multiplication argmax; rendered `n/d`, never decimal). (5) Per-tx prompt build derives `market_ticker_str` from `snap.price_index` + queries `prompt_balance` from `bus.sequencer.q_snapshot().economic_state_t.balances_t` (replaces legacy `snap.get_balance`; the `f64` is the `prompt.rs` render contract — `prompt.rs` is not a TB-14 module surface). (6) Boltzmann selector call replaced: `boltzmann_select_parent(&snap.tape, &prices, &params, &mut rng)` → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2(&snap.price_index, &snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut rng).map(|tx| tx.0)`. Predicate-blind (CR-14.1 + halt-trigger #1) — purely a scheduling priority pick. Predecessor TB-12 Atom 0.5(a) hash 8f1b97f1 superseded. (2026-05-03; architect 2026-05-03 ruling §1.1 + §8 Atom 0.5; TB-11 G3/G4 carry-forward closure): added 4 EvidenceCapsule counters (tb11_lean_error_count / tb11_sorry_block_count / tb11_protocol_parse_failure_count / tb11_partial_accept_count) instrumented at existing classify_lean_error / classify_parse_error / step_partial_ok call sites; sorry-block vs lean-error split via err_detail.contains("sorry") || err_detail.contains("forbidden_payload"); MAX_TX exhausted exit point (inside `if let Some(bundle) = chaintape_bundle`, BEFORE bundle.shutdown) now writes EvidenceCapsule to CAS via runtime::evidence_capsule::write_evidence_capsule + emits TerminalSummary on-chain via runtime::adapter::tb11_emit_terminal_summary_for_run with capsule_cid; bundle.shutdown drains the queued TerminalSummary tx via apply_one. Mirror of TB-8 tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify pattern. Predecessor TB-10 Atom 1+3 hash a5b30cd1 superseded.

Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E hash 3e770c4b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E (2026-05-05; tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible eligibility-boundary semantic-fix): the original TB-16.x.2.2 helper used `if elapsed <= window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` — i.e. require elapsed > delta. With delta=0 the helper required ≥1 round to pass, contradicting its own docstring claim "delta=0 makes every Open case immediately eligible". Smoke r3 (post-Patch-A) showed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE batch returning count=0 because q.q_t.current_round is NOT auto-advanced per-tx (unlike tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible's caller-supplied current_logical_t which auto-advances per-tx); the OMEGA-Confirm path opens the case and scans within the same round, so elapsed=0 for delta=0 was always skipped. Patch E flips the boundary to `if elapsed < window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` (require elapsed >= delta), aligning impl with docstring. Sole callers of this helper today are the FORCE_-prefixed arena hooks; no production caller depends on the prior off-by-one boundary. Docstring updated to reflect `>= delta` and to clarify the time-unit difference vs tb11. Returns (count, bonds_released_micro). emit_system_tx for ChallengeResolve does NOT pre-check case existence/status (sequencer.rs:2590 — construction is unconditional); dispatch-time AlreadyResolved/ChallengeNotFound surfaces as L4.E rejection, not Err here. G1 equation populated WITHOUT alteration per Codex Q1+Q4 remediation: `evaluator_reported_completed_llm_calls == l4_work_attempt_count + l4e_work_attempt_count` (clean halt) + auxiliary `expected + aborted == l4 + l4e` (abort halt). Drain barrier honored via existing ChaintapeBundle::shutdown() — no new Sequencer public method (avoids redundant Class-4 sequencer touch). Predecessor TB-7R hash 87323282 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-7R parent_tx state ruling (2026-05-02): added pub enum ParentTxState (4 variants) + ChainDerivedRunFacts.parent_tx_state field + WorkTxAttempt accumulator + compute_parent_tx_state helper. handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6512:   350	"src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs" = "f305f621702e61f96792c5dbe8666a2cf8ad42e45015b05a71454d54b558db10"  # rehashed by Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 R3.5 (2026-05-08; charter STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2 + SG-A3.2): flush_jsonl_record now reads TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH and, when set, advances refs/chaintape/l4e per L4.E append by creating a deterministic git2 commit anchoring the canonical JSONL bytes. NO change to RejectedSubmissionRecord schema or compute_hash byte stream — additive ref-side attestation only. handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6533:   222	"src/runtime/adapter.rs" = "48da399a63214e68d1c10b7837116ae67d4d8bf68ee32d7ab2c34ca77b1caee3"  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.3 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.3 — CompleteSetRedeem env-var trigger): ADDITIVE — added make_real_complete_set_redeem_signed_by(keypairs, parent_state_root, event_task, owner, outcome, share_units, suffix, timestamp_logical) helper. Mirrors make_real_complete_set_mint_signed_by shape: builds CompleteSetRedeemTx + canonical-digest + agent-sign via AgentKeypairRegistry. Sole caller is the FORCE_REDEEM env-var hook in evaluator.rs (TB-16.x.2.3); no production caller wired yet. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 hash c1360a73 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 (2026-05-05; doc-only follow-up addressing Codex CHALLENGE E.3 from the .fix Class 3 audit): the prior .fix Patch E docstring referenced a nonexistent `Sequencer::set_current_round_for_test` symbol while explaining the time-unit difference between this helper and tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible. Patch F4 corrects the doc to point at the actual in-tree mutator (the `seed_q_for_challenge` test helper at src/state/sequencer.rs:~4185) and clarifies that no production round-advance mechanism is wired yet. NO behavior change — pure docstring edit; helper signature, eligibility logic, and call surface are unchanged from .fix Patch E. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E hash 3e770c4b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E (2026-05-05; tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible eligibility-boundary semantic-fix): the original TB-16.x.2.2 helper used `if elapsed <= window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` — i.e. require elapsed > delta. With delta=0 the helper required ≥1 round to pass, contradicting its own docstring claim "delta=0 makes every Open case immediately eligible". Smoke r3 (post-Patch-A) showed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE batch returning count=0 because q.q_t.current_round is NOT auto-advanced per-tx (unlike tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible's caller-supplied current_logical_t which auto-advances per-tx); the OMEGA-Confirm path opens the case and scans within the same round, so elapsed=0 for delta=0 was always skipped. Patch E flips the boundary to `if elapsed < window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` (require elapsed >= delta), aligning impl with docstring. Sole callers of this helper today are the FORCE_-prefixed arena hooks; no production caller depends on the prior off-by-one boundary. Docstring updated to reflect `>= delta` and to clarify the time-unit difference vs tb11. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash a9bbb1ac superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE — added tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible(seq, window_delta_logical_t, default_resolution) helper. Mirrors tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible shape: scan economic_state_t.challenge_cases_t for entries with status=Open AND q.q_t.current_round - case.opened_at_round > window_delta; emit ChallengeResolveTx via emit_system_tx (SystemEmitCommand::ChallengeResolve) for each eligible case. Returns (count, bonds_released_micro). emit_system_tx for ChallengeResolve does NOT pre-check case existence/status (sequencer.rs:2590 — construction is unconditional); dispatch-time AlreadyResolved/ChallengeNotFound surfaces as L4.E rejection, not Err here. Predecessor TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 hash 3390205f superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 (2026-05-04; architect §7.3 FR-16.3 + FR-16.4): ADDITIVE — added 3 real-signature constructors for arena drivers: make_real_challengetx_signed_by (ChallengeTx; FR-16.3), make_real_market_seed_signed_by (MarketSeedTx; FR-16.4), make_real_complete_set_mint_signed_by (CompleteSetMintTx; FR-16.4). Mirror existing make_real_worktx_signed_by / make_real_verifytx_signed_by patterns. No behavior change to existing helpers. Predecessor TB-11 Atom 4 hash dcdb2566 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-11 Atom 4 (2026-05-02; architect §6.2 ruling): added tb11_emit_terminal_summary_for_run + tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible runtime helpers. Mirror tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify pattern; thin wrappers over Sequencer::emit_system_tx with policy-gated eligibility scan for TaskExpire (state ∈ {Open, Bankrupt} + opened_at_logical_t deadline elapsed + no Finalized claim). Predecessor TB-10 Atom 1 hash 403ff876 superseded.

Both with TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan doc-comments per R-015. Pure additive report-side helpers, no chain side effects. Predecessor Constitution Landing 2026-05-08 hash ce084a43 superseded.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6540:273:"src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs" = "723dadf6dc824b2de37be55cf599d2fedf75ff4ea588be51b549e5eb2d71aff3"  # rehashed by Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 R3 (2026-05-08; charter STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2 / SG-A3.3): CasStore::put now invokes Git2LedgerWriter::advance_chaintape_cas_to(repo_path, git_oid, ...) after durable sidecar append, advancing refs/chaintape/cas to track each new CAS blob OID. Best-effort (`let _ = ...`) so a transient ref-update failure does NOT roll back the durable CAS write — the ref is overwrite-safe (force=true) and recovers on the next put. Pure additive; no change to put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). Predecessor 2026-05-07 list_all_cids hash 687a6144 superseded.  # re-rehashed 2026-05-07 to add TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N34 + FC1-N35 doc-comment backlink to list_all_cids (per R-022 pre-commit hook). Predecessor strict-audit-fix hash e5d0c020 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-C0 strict-audit FC1-INV6 fix (2026-05-07; STRICT_AUDIT_TBC0_TAPE_2026-05-07.md Finding D): NEW pub fn `list_all_cids(&self) -> Vec<Cid>` — sibling to list_cids_by_object_type (R5) / count_by_object_type (R4). Returns every Cid in the in-memory index regardless of object_type. Used by audit_assertions::assert_50_cas_bytes_match_cids to walk all CAS objects + re-hash bytes against stored CIDs (closes the flip_cas_byte detection hole revealed on P05's tape). Pure-additive helper; no disk I/O; no change to put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). Predecessor TB-18R R5 hash e6729db5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18R R5 (2026-05-06; preflight handover/ai-direct/TB-18R_R5_preflight_audit_extension.md §3 + §2.1): NEW pub fn `list_cids_by_object_type(&self, ty: ObjectType) -> Vec<Cid>` — sibling to count_by_object_type (R4). Returns the list of Cids for CAS objects whose metadata `object_type` matches; used by audit_assertions::assert_44_attempt_telemetry_retrievable_from_cas + assert_45_lean_result_retrievable_from_cas to walk all AttemptTelemetry / LeanResult CAS objects per FR-18R.7 / SG-18R.7. Pure-additive helper over in-memory index; no disk I/O. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list). Predecessor TB-18R R4 hash 2d7fd4d5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18R R4 (2026-05-06; preflight handover/ai-direct/TB-18R_R4_STEP_B_invariant.md §3 + §4): NEW pub fn `count_by_object_type(&self, ty: ObjectType) -> u64` — counts CAS objects whose metadata `object_type` matches. Used by chain_derived_run_facts::compute_run_facts_from_chain_with_invariant to populate attempt_aborted_count (FR-18R.4 v2 — count of TerminalAbortRecord CAS objects per run). Pure-additive helper over the in-memory index; no disk I/O; no change to put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list). Predecessor TB-18R R3.fix hash 01884e5b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18R R3.fix (2026-05-06; preflight handover/ai-direct/TB-18R_R3FIX_STEP_B_cas_reload.md §2.1 + §3.1): NEW pub fn `reload_index_from_sidecar(&mut self) -> Result<(), CasError>` — re-reads the on-disk `.turingos_cas_index.jsonl` and merges new entries into self.index. Used by long-lived sequencer.cas to pick up writes performed by short-lived evaluator-side CasStore handles on the same disk path. Idempotent. Reuses existing private `load_index_from_sidecar` free function. Pure-additive; no change to existing put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). Closes the L0-smoke-2026-05-06 split-brain bug where R3 admission helper read sequencer.cas's stale index and fell back to PredicateFailed instead of refining to LeanFailed/SorryBlocked. Predecessor TB-16.x.1 hash 12ce3f35 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.1 (2026-05-04): CasStore::get wraps libgit2 read in worker thread + recv_timeout (default 10s; TURINGOS_CAS_GET_TIMEOUT_SECS knob) + defense-in-depth size-bound check + new CasError::BackendCorruption variant. Closes OBS_TB_16_TAMPER_R2_HANG: libgit2 zlib decompression of back-half-zeroed loose objects (e.g. corrupted MarkovEvidenceCapsule) pegs CPU indefinitely; wrapper converts hang → bounded BackendCorruption error so audit_tape_tamper Round 2 detects 3/3 within 10.3s. Predecessor TB-7.6 hash de86443f superseded.

Returns every Cid in the in-memory index regardless of object_type. Used by audit_assertions::assert_50_cas_bytes_match_cids to walk all CAS objects + re-hash bytes against stored CIDs (closes the flip_cas_byte detection hole revealed on P05's tape). Pure-additive helper; no disk I/O; no change to put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). Predecessor TB-18R R5 hash e6729db5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18R R5 (2026-05-06; preflight handover/ai-direct/TB-18R_R5_preflight_audit_extension.md §3 + §2.1): NEW pub fn `list_cids_by_object_type(&self, ty: ObjectType) -> Vec<Cid>` — sibling to count_by_object_type (R4). Returns the list of Cids for CAS objects whose metadata `object_type` matches; used by audit_assertions::assert_44_attempt_telemetry_retrievable_from_cas + assert_45_lean_result_retrievable_from_cas to walk all AttemptTelemetry / LeanResult CAS objects per FR-18R.7 / SG-18R.7. Pure-additive helper over in-memory index; no disk I/O. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list). Predecessor TB-18R R4 hash 2d7fd4d5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18R R4 (2026-05-06; preflight handover/ai-direct/TB-18R_R4_STEP_B_invariant.md §3 + §4): NEW pub fn `count_by_object_type(&self, ty: ObjectType) -> u64` — counts CAS objects whose metadata `object_type` matches. Used by chain_derived_run_facts::compute_run_facts_from_chain_with_invariant to populate attempt_aborted_count (FR-18R.4 v2 — count of TerminalAbortRecord CAS objects per run). Pure-additive helper over the in-memory index; no disk I/O; no change to put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list). Predecessor TB-18R R3.fix hash 01884e5b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18R R3.fix (2026-05-06; preflight handover/ai-direct/TB-18R_R3FIX_STEP_B_cas_reload.md §2.1 + §3.1): NEW pub fn `reload_index_from_sidecar(&mut self) -> Result<(), CasError>` — re-reads the on-disk `.turingos_cas_index.jsonl` and merges new entries into self.index. Used by long-lived sequencer.cas to pick up writes performed by short-lived evaluator-side CasStore handles on the same disk path. Idempotent. Reuses existing private `load_index_from_sidecar` free function. Pure-additive; no change to existing put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). Closes the L0-smoke-2026-05-06 split-brain bug where R3 admission helper read sequencer.cas's stale index and fell back to PredicateFailed instead of refining to LeanFailed/SorryBlocked. Predecessor TB-16.x.1 hash 12ce3f35 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.1 (2026-05-04): CasStore::get wraps libgit2 read in worker thread + recv_timeout (default 10s; TURINGOS_CAS_GET_TIMEOUT_SECS knob) + defense-in-depth size-bound check + new CasError::BackendCorruption variant. Closes OBS_TB_16_TAMPER_R2_HANG: libgit2 zlib decompression of back-half-zeroed loose objects (e.g. corrupted MarkovEvidenceCapsule) pegs CPU indefinitely; wrapper converts hang → bounded BackendCorruption error so audit_tape_tamper Round 2 detects 3/3 within 10.3s. Predecessor TB-7.6 hash de86443f superseded.

Pure additive — C1 baseline (refs/transitions/main) preserved as backward-compat alias per CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.6. Both with TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan doc-comments per R-015. Pure additive report-side helpers, no chain side effects. Predecessor Constitution Landing 2026-05-08 hash ce084a43 superseded.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6554:273:"src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs" = "723dadf6dc824b2de37be55cf599d2fedf75ff4ea588be51b549e5eb2d71aff3"  # rehashed by Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 R3 (2026-05-08; charter STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2 / SG-A3.3): CasStore::put now invokes Git2LedgerWriter::advance_chaintape_cas_to(repo_path, git_oid, ...) after durable sidecar append, advancing refs/chaintape/cas to track each new CAS blob OID. Best-effort (`let _ = ...`) so a transient ref-update failure does NOT roll back the durable CAS write — the ref is overwrite-safe (force=true) and recovers on the next put. Pure additive; no change to put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6556:350:"src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs" = "f305f621702e61f96792c5dbe8666a2cf8ad42e45015b05a71454d54b558db10"  # rehashed by Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 R3.5 (2026-05-08; charter STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2 + SG-A3.2): flush_jsonl_record now reads TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH and, when set, advances refs/chaintape/l4e per L4.E append by creating a deterministic git2 commit anchoring the canonical JSONL bytes. Best-effort hook (failures don't roll back JSONL append). Per FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.6 pre-Stage-A3 evidence remains JSONL-only and replayable. NO change to RejectedSubmissionRecord schema or compute_hash byte stream — additive ref-side attestation only. Class 3 surface per `feedback_class4_cannot_hide_in_class3` (rejection_evidence.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md §12 STEP_B restricted list). Predecessor TB-18R R3 hash 50971e14 superseded.

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6859:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5107:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6860:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6886:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6749:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6887:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6752:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6922:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:4601:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12860:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:22:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6929:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:5259:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7726:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6852:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6940:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:5333:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8679:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5275:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6952:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:5638:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9577:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7105:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation in CI without a live API. **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6953:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:5644:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9914:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9339:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6969:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_15_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-04_R1.md:3949:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:27:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:6984:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5408:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:7098:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10039:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:7111:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10767:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5109:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:7123:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:11116:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6114:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8129:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:7239:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:5573:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:7240:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:5576:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:7309:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6114:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8129:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:7311:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6124:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8143:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/evidence/tb_18r_r9_p38_p49_2026-05-06/README.md:50:The runner-script's initial extraction of `EXPECTED_COMPLETED` from PPUT_RESULT used a stale field-name cascade (`completed_llm_calls` → `externalized_llm_calls` → `proposal_count`); none match the v4 PPUT_RESULT shape, so the runner's first invariant write had `expected=0`. The canonical v4 postprocess (`handover/tests/scripts/tb_18r_postprocess_invariant_v4.sh`) extracts the correct count (`step_reject + parse_fail + llm_err + sorry_block + omega + complete + complete_via_tape + preseed_work`; `step_partial_ok` excluded per R3 §1.3 amended CAS-only path) and rewrites `chain_invariant.json` with the proper expected count.

handover/evidence/tb_15_markov_capsule_2026-05-04/README.md:54:R3 fix: store the EXACT prelim bytes in CAS (with capsule_id+sha256 zeroed). `capsule_id = sha256(stored_bytes)`. CAS keys by sha256(stored_bytes) = capsule_id. `cas.get(&capsule_id)` succeeds; consumer canonical-decodes + restores capsule_id+sha256 from `Cid::from_content(&retrieved_bytes)`.

Position taken: charter's "verify boltzmann_select_parent_v2 is called in WorkTx admission path" is satisfied by the existing call at evaluator.rs:1828 (`_v2_canonical_pick`); the parent_tx record needed for SG-16.x.2.4 lives in ProposalTelemetry (CAS object, proposal-time data) — sequencer-side admission has no parent_tx field on WorkTx.
