handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:4:**Status**: **PARTIALLY FIXED 2026-05-07** (Bug 1 + Bug 3 fixed in commit chain; Bug 2 escalated). See §10 for fix log.
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:12:The TB-C0 constitution-gate test `fc1_attempt_count_equals_tape_count` PASSES on synthetic data (test scaffold's hand-crafted `ChainDerivedRunFacts` correctly fires `NegativeDelta` for the canonical TB-18 P49 N→1 collapse shape). But the **EMPIRICAL FC1-INV3 invariant fails on real workload** — `handover/evidence/tb_18r_phase_3_2026-05-06T14-13-55Z/PHASE_3_BATCH_SUMMARY.json` shows 7/7 problems return `Err(...)` on the binary, despite the architect §5 #1 direct check passing on 5/7.
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:14:The structural test is correct; the **empirical implementation has 3 distinct bugs** that prevent the canonical FC1 hard-invariant equation from being computed from real chain artifacts. This OBS catalogs all three.
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:44:**Fix**: derive `expected_completed_attempts` from the evaluator's PPUT `tool_dist` field — specifically the count of `step` outcomes that produced an LLM call (excluding `omega_wtool` double-count). Or surface a new evaluator field `externalized_llm_cycle_count` that is the canonical LHS quantity.
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:59:- (A) Filter out synthetic-gate L4.E entries from `l4e_work_attempt_count`. Requires the synthetic-gate variant to be type-distinguishable from real rejections (a new `RejectionClass::SyntheticGate` discriminant or `is_synthetic: bool` field).
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:63:**Recommendation**: (A) — preserve canonical equation; add typed discriminator to L4.E.
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:65:**Class**: 3 (touches `RejectionClass` enum + chain_derived counting). May be 4 if the discriminator change requires canonical-signing-payload mutation (architect verdict).
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:82:**Class**: 4 — bumps canonical `ChainDerivedRunFacts` schema (+1 serde field) + canonical equation. Per `feedback_class4_cannot_hide_in_class3`, this is STEP_B-restricted (architect ratification + parallel-branch protocol required).
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:89:- No fixture for the synthetic-gate-on-omega case (Bug 2)
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:117:The structural test in `tests/constitution_fc1_runtime_loop.rs::fc1_every_externalized_attempt_is_tape_visible` correctly fires on the canonical TB-18 P49 N→1 collapse shape (32 expected, 1 chain → NegativeDelta delta=-31). It catches the WORST-CASE failure mode that motivated TB-18R.
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:140:## §10 Fix log (2026-05-07 round-5; per user "你先修复" directive)
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:145:| **Bug 2** (synthetic L4.E gate inflates l4e_count) | 3 | 🟡 **ESCALATED** | Requires sequencer.rs change (synthetic-gate discriminator) + chain_derived counting filter. `src/state/sequencer.rs` is in CLAUDE.md Code Standard STEP_B file list. Per `feedback_class4_cannot_hide_in_class3` + STEP_B protocol: NOT fixed in this round-5; needs separate STEP_B parallel-branch + dual audit. Forward-bound; document and request architect ratification. **Empirical impact**: P03/P06/P07 (1-shot omega) show `delta=1` from this; P05 has `delta=1` from a mix of Bug 2 + a single overcounted attempt. |
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:146:| **Bug 3** (missing `capsule_anchored_attempt_count` 3-term) | 4 → reclassified to 3 with explicit deviation stance | ✅ FIXED | `src/runtime/chain_derived_run_facts.rs` adds `pub capsule_anchored_attempt_count: u64` with `#[serde(default)]` for backward-compat. `compute_run_facts_from_chain_with_invariant` walks CAS for AttemptTelemetry records with `outcome == AttemptOutcome::PartialAccepted` (variant 6, Phase 2 CAS-only per §3.2 + R3 §1.3). `delta` formula extended to `l4 + l4e + capsule_anchored - expected`. **Deviation stance** (`feedback_architect_deviation_stance`): not Class 4 STEP_B because (a) `chain_derived_run_facts.rs` is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted file list (only sequencer.rs + typed_tx.rs + cas/schema.rs + bus.rs + kernel.rs + wallet.rs are), (b) field is strictly additive with `#[serde(default)]` — backward-compat with all pre-fix evidence, (c) NOT a canonical-signing-payload mutation — `ChainDerivedRunFacts` is a runtime-derived facts struct, not a typed-tx schema. Trust-root rehash recorded in `genesis_payload.toml`. **Empirical verification**: re-ran `tb_18r_compute_invariant` on P05's tape → `capsule_anchored_attempt_count: 8` (matches the `step_partial_ok=8` observation). 3-term equation `0 + 13 + 8 = 21` vs expected `20` → delta=1 (Bug 2 residue). |
handover/alignment/OBS_TBC0_FC1_INV3_THREE_BUGS_2026-05-06.md:152:| FC1-INV6 flip_cas_byte hole on P05 | 3 | ✅ FIXED | New `assert_50_cas_bytes_match_cids` in `src/runtime/audit_assertions.rs` Layer B walks all CAS objects via new `CasStore::list_all_cids` helper, re-hashes content via `Cid::from_content`, asserts byte-for-byte CID match. Re-ran `audit_tape_tamper` on P05 → **detected 3/3 (was 2/3 pre-fix)**. Trust-root rehash recorded. |

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:38:> 3. (P1 Exit 8 — *as primitives*) `AcceptedLedger::load_from_path` reconstructs the canonical `state_root` from L4 only AND verifies the chain end-to-end (P0-4 fail-closed default);
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:72:**WalletTool framing (sharpened post-audit 2026-04-29 CF-3)**: `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is **NOT a legacy mutable adapter** — it is a *read-only projection* of `QState.economic_state_t.balances_t`. Mutations to economic state happen exclusively through the canonical RSP path (`SettlementEngine` / `EscrowVault` / `StakeManager` / `monetary_invariant`). No new RSP code may depend on `WalletTool.credit()` or on `WalletTool` mutating its `HashMap<String, f64>` to represent canonical balance state. Existing `WalletTool` tests stay temporarily as legacy behavior tests; they get removed or rewritten as RSP-1/RSP-2 lands.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-2_charter_2026-04-30.md:18:- is committed by `Sequencer::apply_one` to the **canonical L4 transition ledger** (`bottom_white::ledger::transition_ledger` + `LedgerWriter`, NOT `economy::ledger::AcceptedLedger`);

V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo: agent-callable system_tx surface count unchanged at zero; tick is policy-gated, not agent-discretion) + `WP-§5.L4` (RunExhausted + TaskExpire + TaskBankruptcy in canonical L4) + `WP-§14.1` (TypedTx additive bumps: TerminalSummary + TaskExpire extended; TaskBankruptcy NEW — no agent-callable surface) + `WP-§19` (RSP-3.2 capital liberation precondition).

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:7:**Phase**: P2 primary (durable agent identity closes the run-local-only caveat carried by TB-7), P3 carry-forward (collapses legacy `WalletTool` f64 mutator path so `EconomicState.balances_t` becomes the **single canonical source of agent balance**).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:11:**FC-trace** (constitution articles): `Art.I.1` (closes 5-step compile loop's identity provenance — every Proposal carries an identity that survives across runs, prerequisite for Capability Compilation) + `Art.III.4` (no fake accepted; on-chain registry binds AgentId↔public-key so signatures cannot be impersonated cross-run) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx surface gains `AgentRegisterTx` variant) + `WP-§5.L4` (registry transitions live on canonical L4) + `feedback_launch_priority` (durable identity precedes Lean Proof Task Market MVP per ruling 13).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:22:  P3:RSP-1-projection (Wallet collapses to read-only projection; EconomicState.balances_t canonical)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:40:TB-7 / TB-7R wired per-LLM-proposal Ed25519 signatures into the canonical L4 path. **The keypair lifecycle is run-local** (process memory, `runtime_repo/agent_pubkeys.json` written per run, fail-closed-on-existing). Architect ruling 2026-05-01 D2 explicitly forbade calling this "durable identity" — it is *run-local identity, not durable reputation identity*.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:46:3. **`verify_chaintape` cross-run replay** currently rebuilds AgentId → pubkey from a per-run manifest file. A reviewer who only has the L4 ChainTape cannot independently verify a WorkTx signature without the manifest sidecar — i.e., **the canonical path is not yet self-contained**.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:48:Concurrently, the runtime carries a parallel f64 ledger in `WalletTool` (`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs:24`) that pre-dates `EconomicState.balances_t` (introduced CO P2.0). Every dispatch arm since TB-3 has mutated `EconomicState.balances_t` (MicroCoin); the legacy `WalletTool.balances` (f64) is mutated only by `bus.rs` market shares code (the v3 simulation path that ChainTape does not touch). **Two ledgers, one canonical chain** — this is the literal definition of `feedback_proxy_running_vs_source_drift`-style trap waiting for the next reviewer to step on it.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:50:Per architect directive 2026-05-02 ruling 13 + LATEST §9: TB-9 closes both gaps in one bundle. Durable AgentRegistry **and** Wallet Projection are co-located because they share the same invariant — one canonical record, replayable from L4, no shadow ledger.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:60:  Every AgentId observed at WorkTx-signing time is registered exactly once on canonical L4

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-8_charter_2026-05-02.md:349:If TB-8 ships clean, the highest-value next slot is **TB-9 = Durable AgentRegistry + Wallet Projection**. TB-7R + TB-8 leave the system with run-local agent identity; TB-9 makes it persistent. Without durable identity, future NodeMarket position ownership and TB-10 user-facing payout addresses are not stable. Alongside the durable registry, `WalletTool` collapses to a read-only projection (no f64 mutation; `EconomicState` remains canonical).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-8_charter_2026-05-02.md:367:## §10 Cross-references
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:1:# TB-3 Charter — DRAFT v2 — P3 RSP-1 Formal Tx Surface (WP-canonical)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:4:**Status**: DRAFT v2 (incorporates WP-canonical reconciliation per user verdict 2026-04-30; STEP_B Phase-0 NOT yet launched; no production code touched.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:7:**Supersedes**: DRAFT v1 of this file (3-variant proposal with WorkTx schema bump). Rewritten after re-reading WP v2 + WP economic supplement; user verdict confirms WP-canonical shape.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:13:Formalize RSP-1 admission as **two first-class TypedTx variants** on the canonical L4 — `TaskOpenTx` (task metadata registration) and `EscrowLockTx` (sole bounty funding path). **Delete** the TB-2 P0-B option (a) bridge at `src/state/sequencer.rs:205-215`. `WorkTx.stake` stays inline per WP § 14.1 + § 18 Invariant 5; **no `YesStakeTx` variant is introduced**. After TB-3, WorkTx admission is structural (looks up real ChainTape-recorded escrow + checks inline stake against agent solvency), not bridged from a synthetic `TxId(task_id.0)` lookup, and accepted WorkTx commits stake by debiting balances → stakes_t in a single conserved transition.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:21:- **§ 3 P3 Forbidden CF-2 — "no ghost liquidity without explicit treasury debit"** — discharged structurally: every increase to `task_markets_t[task_id].total_escrow` is the consequence of a single `EscrowLockTx` whose canonical L4 row debits `balances_t[sponsor]` by the same amount in one conserved transition.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:24:**Note on canonical numbering vs user mental model**: the user's preview cited "P3:6 (task_market into ChainTape) + P3:8 (task_open_tx is admission entry)". Per ROADMAP § 3 numbering, P3:6 = "predicate-pass yields only provisional" (RSP-3 territory) and P3:8 = "settlement payout ≤ escrow" (RSP-4 territory). The user's intent — structural ChainTape-recorded admission — is captured above as the CF-2 forbidden-list discharges, not as numbered Exit advancement. **TB-3 does not flip a new numbered P3 Exit RED→GREEN**; it formalizes the surface that TB-2 admitted via bridge.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:37:## 3. WP-canonical decision blocks (must persist verbatim through any charter revision)

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:41:TB-3 follows the whitepaper-canonical implementation shape, NOT the literal Roadmap § 3 transaction list:
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:108:This is the WP-canonical interpretation: stake is real money locked at accept; rejection records evidence only; future explicit accepted transitions resolve the lock. It is consistent with Law 2 ("Only Investment Costs Money"), § 18 Invariants 4 + 5 + 6 + 7, and economic § 7's "押注".

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:392:> Submit `TaskOpenTx`(task_id=T) then `EscrowLockTx`(task_id=T, sponsor=S, amount=A) → two canonical L4 rows, zero L4.E. After: `task_markets_t[T].total_escrow == A`, `escrows_t[escrow_lock_tx_id] == EscrowEntry { amount: A, depositor: S, task_id: T }`, `balances_t[S]` decremented by A. `assert_total_ctf_conserved(genesis, q_after, &[])` holds across each step. (Tests I20 + I21 + I22.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:395:> After Proof 1 setup, a predicate-passing `WorkTx`(task_id=T, agent=Solver, stake=K) where `balances_t[Solver] >= K` is admitted, advances `state_root_t` to `sha256(WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1 ‖ prev ‖ canonical_hash(work))`, advances `ledger_root_t`, increments `logical_t`, debits `balances_t[Solver]` by K, inserts `stakes_t[work.tx_id] = StakeEntry { amount: K, staker: Solver, task_id: T }`. Each missing precondition (no TaskOpen / no EscrowLock / Solver balance < K / stake==0) routes the WorkTx to L4.E with the appropriate class WITHOUT mutating economic_state. The bridge code at `sequencer.rs:205-215` is deleted in the same commit; `bridge_pattern_does_not_resurrect_in_src` test passes. (Tests I23 + I24 + I25 + I26 + I27 + I28.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:398:> Reconstructing `QState` from canonical L4 alone (extending TB-2's `replay_full_transition` with the 2 new TxKind arms) yields the same `economic_state_t` as the live sequencer, including `balances_t`, `escrows_t`, `stakes_t`, and `task_markets_t` (with `total_escrow` re-derived correctly). A property test over 1000 random valid sequences confirms `assert_total_ctf_conserved(genesis, q_after, &[])` AND `task_market.total_escrow == Σ escrows[e].amount where e.task_id matches` hold at every step. (Tests I29 + I30.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:402:> RSP-1 formal tx surface is on the canonical L4. `TaskOpenTx` and `EscrowLockTx` are first-class TypedTx variants. WorkTx admission is structural; bridge is deleted. WorkTx.stake commits real money on accept; rejected WorkTx leaves economic state untouched. `task_market.total_escrow` is a derived cache, not a money holding.

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:1:# TB-4 Charter — DRAFT v2 — P3 RSP-2 Verifier Bond + Challenger NO Stake (WP-canonical, narrow scope)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:6:**Predecessor**: TB-3 SHIPPED 2026-04-30 (merge `e99b158`; ship_commits `9af6d80..e99b158`); TB-3 production claim = "RSP-1 formal tx surface is on the canonical L4. `TaskOpenTx` and `EscrowLockTx` are first-class TypedTx variants. WorkTx admission is structural; bridge deleted. WorkTx.stake commits real money on accept; rejected WorkTx leaves economic state untouched."
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:49:## 3. WP-canonical decision blocks (must persist verbatim through any charter revision)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:51:### 3.1 WP-canonical implementation shape
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:53:TB-4 follows the whitepaper-canonical implementation shape, NOT the literal Roadmap § 3 transaction list. Per the **WP-vs-Roadmap reconciliation rule** codified in `feedback_wp_vs_roadmap_reconciliation` (TB-3 precedent: WorkTx.stake stays inline; no `YesStakeTx` variant) and re-affirmed by directive 2026-04-30 § 2:
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:60:  - `no_stake_tx` ↔ semantic role of `ChallengeTx.stake` (the WP-canonical NO position; "Challenger Agent 提交 challenge_tx 押注 NO_E" — economic § 7).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:78:- Verifier verdict (`Confirm | Doubt`) is **NOT** stored in Q_t; it is recoverable from canonical L4 (the `VerifyTx.verdict` field of the L4 row). RSP-3's settlement engine will index it from L4 replay; no Q_t mutation needed in TB-4.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:102:This is an **entry-shape additive change**, NOT a new sub-field. Pre-TB-4 serialized rows deserialize with the empty TxId. (No pre-TB-4 ChallengeCase rows exist on canonical L4 since the Challenge arm was `NotYetImplemented`.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:110:3. `verify.target_work_tx` exists in canonical L4 — else `TargetNotFound` (per § 3.8).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:124:state_root_t = sha256(VERIFY_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1 ‖ prev ‖ canonical_hash(verify))
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:127:Per § 3.10: **the `verdict` field rides canonical L4 only**. Q_t is never mutated based on Confirm/Doubt; the verdict is a signed economic signal, not subjective truth.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:135:3. `challenge.target_work_tx` exists in canonical L4 — else `TargetNotFound`.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:151:state_root_t = sha256(CHALLENGE_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1 ‖ prev ‖ canonical_hash(challenge))
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:184:| `TargetNotFound` | `target_work_tx` does not exist in canonical L4 (no row with this TxId was ever accepted as a WorkTx). Distinguishes "you addressed nothing" from "you addressed something inactive". |
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:212:The `VerifyVerdict` field (`Confirm | Doubt`) is preserved in the wire schema and rides canonical L4. Three explicit non-actions:
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:290:All arms remain pure (no I/O); apply_one writes the canonical L4 row. Three-class error taxonomy (§ 3.8) embedded:
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:296:  3. canonical-L4 lookup: target_work_tx ∈ accepted L4 rows? else TargetNotFound.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:308:  8. q_next.state_root_t = sha256(b"turingosv4.verify.accept.v1" ‖ prev_root ‖ canonical_hash(tx)).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:313:  3. canonical-L4 lookup: target_work_tx ∈ accepted L4 rows? else TargetNotFound.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:327:  9. q_next.state_root_t = sha256(b"turingosv4.challenge.accept.v1" ‖ prev_root ‖ canonical_hash(tx)).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:330:**Note on step 3 (canonical-L4 lookup)**: `dispatch_transition` is pure and operates on `q: &QState`; it does not have a direct L4 reader. The TargetNotFound check is performed by reading `q.economic_state_t.stakes_t.0.contains_key(&target)` OR `q.q_t.agents[..].last_accepted_tx == target` lookup OR (cleanest) by introducing a small helper on Q_t that reports "target_work_tx was ever accepted" via a per-tx marker in Q_t or an additional read of `stakes_t` ∪ "stakes_t entries that have been deleted by RSP-3 finalize" (empty in TB-4). **In TB-4 minimum scope, since RSP-3 has not yet introduced finalize-removes-stakes_t logic, `stakes_t.contains_key` is a sufficient proxy for "ever accepted as live WorkTx" — making `TargetNotFound` and `TargetWorkInactive` collapse to the same condition.** Atom 2 / Atom 4-5 detailed design will distinguish the two via the L4 lookup channel (e.g., a parallel "all-accepted-tx-ids" set on Q_t, OR by deferring `TargetNotFound` to RSP-3 when finalize creates the distinction). **Open Atom-1 design question** — see § 7 Atom 1 deliverables.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:339:- `TargetNotFound` — Verify/Challenge.target_work_tx is not in canonical L4. Per directive Q3 three-class taxonomy.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:374:- `verify_tx_canonical_digest_includes_parent_state_root` (T1)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:375:- `challenge_tx_canonical_digest_includes_parent_state_root` (T2)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:393:- `submit_verify_tx_appends_to_canonical_l4_and_locks_bond` (I31)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:394:- `submit_challenge_tx_appends_to_canonical_l4_and_opens_case` (I32)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:430:5. **No new TypedTx variants.** No `NoStakeTx` / no `VerifierBondTx` / no `ChallengeStakeTx` / no `VerifierStakeTx` / no `ProvisionalAcceptTx` / no `ChallengeResolveTx` / no `SettlementTx` / no `SlashTx` (per § 3.1 WP-canonical + RSP-3/4 boundary; CI-enforced by I44).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:492:  • apply_one accepted-spine path for Verify (CAS put + canonical L4 append).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:534:> Submit `TaskOpenTx`(task_id=T) → `EscrowLockTx`(task_id=T, sponsor=S, amount=A) → `WorkTx`(task_id=T, agent=Solver, stake=K, predicate-passing) → `VerifyTx`(target_work_tx=Solver_tx, verifier=V, bond=B, verdict=Confirm) where `balances_t[V] >= B`. Result: four canonical L4 rows, zero L4.E. After: `stakes_t[Solver_tx] == StakeEntry { K, Solver, T }` (TB-3 lock-on-accept; unchanged), `stakes_t[Verify_tx] == StakeEntry { B, V, T }` (NEW), `balances_t[V]` decremented by B, `balances_t[Solver]` decremented by K. `assert_total_ctf_conserved(genesis, q_after, &[])` holds across each step. (Tests I31 + I33 + I35 + I37.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:537:> After Proof 1 setup, submit `ChallengeTx`(target_work_tx=Solver_tx, challenger=C, stake=N, counterexample_cid=≠Cid::ZERO) where `balances_t[C] >= N`. Result: one canonical L4 row, zero L4.E. After: `challenge_cases_t[Challenge_tx] == ChallengeCase { challenger: C, bond: N, opened_at_round: q.q_t.current_round_at_accept, target_work_tx: Solver_tx }`, `balances_t[C]` decremented by N. A second `ChallengeTx`(target_work_tx=Solver_tx, challenger=C2, stake=N2, ...) — DIFFERENT challenger, SAME target — also accepts as a distinct challenge_cases_t row. Each missing precondition (target inactive / stake==0 / counterexample == ZERO / balance < N) routes the ChallengeTx to L4.E with the appropriate three-class TransitionError WITHOUT mutating economic_state. (Tests I32 + I34 + I36 + I38 + I39.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:540:> Reconstructing `QState` from canonical L4 alone (extending TB-3's `replay_full_transition` with the 2 new accepted-spine arms) yields the same `economic_state_t` as the live sequencer, including `balances_t`, `stakes_t` (now multi-role: YES + verifier-bond), `challenge_cases_t` (NEW: bond + opened_at_round + target_work_tx). A property test over 1000 random valid sequences of `TaskOpen + EscrowLock + Work + Verify + Challenge` confirms `assert_total_ctf_conserved(genesis, q_after, &[])` AND `task_market.total_escrow == Σ escrows[e].amount` AND **`challenge_cases_t[c].opened_at_round == q.q_t.current_round at accept time`** hold at every step. The window-anchor pinpoint test asserts the exact equality `opened_at_round == sequencer.q.q_t.current_round_at_dispatch_for(challenge_tx_id)`. The no-drift CI test asserts no `NoStakeTx` / `VerifierBondTx` / `ChallengeStakeTx` / `VerifierStakeTx` literal exists in src/ (per directive § 5.1). (Tests I41 + I42 + I43 + I44.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:544:> RSP-2 admission spine is on the canonical L4. `VerifyTx` debits a bond into stakes_t; `ChallengeTx` opens a ChallengeCase with the challenge-window structural anchor `opened_at_round`. The 9-sub-field EconomicState invariant and 5-holding CTF invariant are preserved without modification. Slashing, provisional reward, settlement, and challenge-window CLOSURE remain RSP-3+ territory. Verifier verdicts ride canonical L4 (signal+stake, not subjective judge). No NoStakeTx / VerifierBondTx variants exist in src.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-4_charter_2026-04-30.md:554:**STEP_B Phase-0** (Atom 1; after v2 sign-off): narrow dual audit (Codex + Gemini) on charter v2 + preflight. Scope: WP-canonical reconciliation correctness; schema bump correctness; forbidden-list completeness; three-class error taxonomy soundness; multi-challenger representability shape; § 3 ten decision blocks each line-traceable to WP / ROADMAP / directive. Conservative-merge verdict (VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS). Round-cap = 2.

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:311:### 5.3 Tests — split battery (AMENDED post Codex round-2 Q6 CHALLENGE; canonical source-of-truth = preflight § 8)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:317:  - T1 `challenge_resolve_canonical_digest_is_deterministic`
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:351:  - I70 `submit_challenge_resolve_released_appends_to_canonical_l4`
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:461:> An agent (or any external caller) attempting to submit `ChallengeResolveTx` / `FinalizeRewardTx` / `TaskExpireTx` / `TerminalSummaryTx` through `Sequencer::submit_agent_tx` is rejected at ingress with `SystemTxForbiddenOnAgentIngress`. Zero canonical L4 row appended; zero L4.E mutation of `economic_state_t`. The same variants submitted through `Sequencer::emit_system_tx` with valid `system_signature` accept; with invalid signature reject as `InvalidSystemSignatureLive`. (Tests U22-U25 + I45-I49.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:464:> Sequence: TaskOpen → EscrowLock → Work (predicate-passing; YES stake locked) → Challenge (NO stake locked into challenge_cases_t) → `emit_system_tx(ChallengeResolveTx{Released, target=Challenge_tx})`. Result: 5 canonical L4 rows, zero L4.E. After resolve: `balances_t[challenger]` returns to its post-Challenge-funding pre-Challenge value; `challenge_cases_t[Challenge_tx].bond == 0` AND `.status == Released` (case preserved as audit trail per directive § 7 Q6); Solver `stakes_t[work.tx_id]` and (if seeded) Verifier `stakes_t[verify.tx_id]` are **unchanged**. CTF holds at every step. (Tests I50-I53.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:467:> `emit_system_tx(ChallengeResolveTx{UpheldDeferred, target=Challenge_tx})` produces 1 canonical L4 row; `economic_state_t` bit-identical to pre-resolve **except** `case.status = UpheldDeferred` (status field flip; bond preserved for TB-6 slash). Multi-challenger: two distinct ChallengeCases against same target; resolve one Released → the other stays Open. Replay-from-canonical-L4 reconstructs the same Q across the 7-tx-kind RSP-3.0/3.1 surface. Anti-drift CI confirms zero `SlashTx` / `SettlementTx` / `ProvisionalAcceptTx` / `ReputationUpdateTx` literals in src/. (Tests I54-I58 + extended I44.)
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:471:> RSP-3.0 substrate is on the canonical runtime: `submit_agent_tx` enforces the agent ingress barrier; `emit_system_tx` is the only path for system-emitted economic mutators; `system_signature` is live-verified at every system-tx accept. RSP-3.1 challenge-resolution surface is on the canonical L4: `ChallengeResolveTx{Released}` closes the CTF round-trip on challenger bond; `ChallengeResolveTx{UpheldDeferred}` reserves the slash-resolution path as a typed marker. Solver YES stake + Verifier bond stay locked PROVISIONAL pending RSP-3.2 (TB-6) slash + RSP-4 (TB-7+) settlement. The 9-sub-field EconomicState invariant + 5-holding CTF invariant are preserved. The four anti-drift renames (resolve ≠ judge / release ≠ settlement / UpheldDeferred ≠ slash / system_signature ≠ schema-only) are CI-enforced.

If dual audit returns CHALLENGE/VETO, the user authorization does NOT cover post-defect ship; the standard remediation cycle applies.

handover/directives/2026-05-01_TB6_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:456:[Archived in repo log via commit body of this file's commit. Two parallel structurally-identical passes were delivered; both convey the same 7-decision verdict with the same atom plan + RSP-M track. Pass 1 = ChatGPT-style headered narrative ("最高优先级裁决" → "最终判断"); Pass 2 = dense sequential transcript ("一句话裁决" → "最终执行口令"). The reconstructed canonical form above merges both faithfully without omission of binding content.]

handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A3_§8_PACKET.md:77:**Step-6 status**: preserved as defense-in-depth. With synchronous `dispatch_transition` and the new Step-4b gate, Step-6 is structurally unreachable for the same WorkTx (4b fires first on identical inequality). Step-6 retained because (a) it is the canonical pre-A3 invariant and rolling it back would be a code-removal regression-risk; (b) it remains the system-side check for any future code path that bypasses the agent-bound dispatch (e.g., system-emitted FinalizeReward with a debit-style downstream — currently not WorkTx-shaped but the helper signature stays correct).
handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A3_§8_PACKET.md:154:**No canonical signing payload change**: `WorkSigningPayload::canonical_digest()` projection is unchanged. The agent's signature continues to cover `(tx_id, task_id, parent_state_root, agent_id, read_set, write_set, proposal_cid, predicate_results, stake, timestamp_logical)` — the same 10 fields as pre-A3. `stake_micro` flows through `WorkTx.stake: StakeMicroCoin` (existing field) so the digest pre-image is identical structure.

handover/proposals/TB-18_ATOM_D_DESIGN_2026-05-05.md:61:**Scope**: Modifies `TaskMarketEntry` canonical schema in `src/state/q_state.rs` + sequencer admission logic in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1167-1188`.
handover/proposals/TB-18_ATOM_D_DESIGN_2026-05-05.md:63:**Per architect Q2 hard rule**: this MODIFIES `task_markets_t` value type → modifies `economic_state_t` shape → modifies `QState` canonical state schema. While the architect Q2 list does NOT explicitly enumerate "QState schema mutation", it DOES include "sequencer admission semantics" — and admission decisions in TaskBankruptcy + TaskExpire + CompleteSetRedeem ALL depend on `task_markets_t[*].state` reads. Changing the value type is a sequencer admission semantics change.
handover/proposals/TB-18_ATOM_D_DESIGN_2026-05-05.md:73:**Scope**: Major canonical schema mutation in `src/state/q_state.rs`. Every Q-state hash recomputes. Existing chains' replay determinism breaks (none of them have `lifecycle_history`; serde-default would produce empty histories that never reproduce the original behavior).
handover/proposals/TB-18_ATOM_D_DESIGN_2026-05-05.md:75:**Class verdict**: **Class 4 — STOP per architect Q2** (canonical state schema + sequencer admission semantics + replay-determinism break for existing chains).

handover/proposals/TB-17_PRE_17_7_INTAPE_MARKOV_DESIGN_2026-05-05.md:169:1. EvidenceCapsule must use `serde_json` canonical encoding (or BCS) where adding `markov_capsule_cid: Option<Cid>` is a non-breaking serde feature change (`#[serde(default)]`).
handover/proposals/TB-17_PRE_17_7_INTAPE_MARKOV_DESIGN_2026-05-05.md:171:3. The audit_tape resolver path must be able to derive: `chain.l4 → find TerminalSummaryTx → cas.get(evidence_capsule_cid) → decode EvidenceCapsule → read markov_capsule_cid → cas.get(markov_capsule_cid) → decode MarkovEvidenceCapsule → use as previous_capsule_cid for the NEXT chain's first capsule`.
handover/proposals/TB-17_PRE_17_7_INTAPE_MARKOV_DESIGN_2026-05-05.md:184:**If verification at implementation time reveals β-A is infeasible** (e.g., EvidenceCapsule canonical encoding does NOT support backward-compatible field add, or TerminalSummaryTx signing payload omits evidence_capsule_cid), AI-coder MUST:

handover/proposals/TB-17_PRE_17_7_INTAPE_MARKOV_DESIGN_2026-05-05.md:285:- Existing chains: their EvidenceCapsules lack `markov_capsule_cid` (decode as `None` via serde-default); resolver returns `Ok(None)` for them — they look like Genesis to β audit_tape, which is **correct** because the inheritance information was simply not written yet.
handover/proposals/TB-17_PRE_17_7_INTAPE_MARKOV_DESIGN_2026-05-05.md:321:1. **β-A feasibility**: confirm `EvidenceCapsule` canonical encoding supports backward-compatible `Option<Cid>` field add via `#[serde(default)]`.

 succeeded in 1ms:
//! TB-9 Atom 1 — Durable agent keystore: encrypted-at-rest persistence of
//! per-agent Ed25519 secrets across evaluator restarts.
//!
//! Mirrors `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs:417-463` exactly:
//! - default path `~/.turingos/keystore/agent_keystore.enc` (env override
//!   `TURINGOS_AGENT_KEYSTORE_PATH`)
//! - Argon2id KDF (m=64MiB, t=3, p=4 default; env-tunable via the same
//!   `TURINGOS_KDF_*` knobs used for the system keystore)
//! - ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD encryption-at-rest
//! - atomic write 0600 (mode bits enforced on Unix)
//! - format magic `TOS4AGTKEY1` (distinct from system's `TOS4SYSKEY1`)
//!
//! Plaintext shape: bincode-encoded `BTreeMap<String, [u8; 32]>` mapping
//! `AgentId.0` → 32-byte Ed25519 secret seed. Public keys are NOT stored —
//! they are recomputed from each seed at load time.
//!
//! TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14 (durable agent identity primitive; satisfies
//! architect TB-9 mandate "agent durable key registry" + "cross-run
//! identity").

use argon2::{Algorithm, Argon2, Params, Version};
use chacha20poly1305::aead::{Aead, KeyInit};
use chacha20poly1305::{ChaCha20Poly1305, Nonce};
use secrecy::{ExposeSecret, SecretString};
use std::collections::BTreeMap;
use std::env;
use std::fmt;
use std::fs::{self, OpenOptions};
use std::io::Write;
use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
use zeroize::Zeroize;

const DEFAULT_KDF_MEMORY_KIB: u32 = 65_536;
const DEFAULT_KDF_ITER: u32 = 3;
const DEFAULT_KDF_LANES: u32 = 4;
const DERIVED_KEY_LEN: usize = 32;
const SECRET_KEY_LEN: usize = 32;
const SALT_LEN: usize = 16;
const NONCE_LEN: usize = 12;
const FORMAT_MAGIC: &[u8; 11] = b"TOS4AGTKEY1";
const FORMAT_VERSION: u8 = 1;

/// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: agent keystore lifecycle / crypto error taxonomy.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub enum AgentKeystoreError {
    Io(std::io::Error),
    Entropy(getrandom::Error),
    KdfParam(String),
    Kdf(argon2::Error),
    Crypto(&'static str),
    InvalidFormat(&'static str),
    HomeUnavailable,
    Bincode(String),
}

impl fmt::Display for AgentKeystoreError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
        match self {
            Self::Io(e) => write!(f, "agent keystore I/O failed: {e}"),
            Self::Entropy(e) => write!(f, "agent keystore entropy failed: {e}"),
            Self::KdfParam(msg) => write!(f, "agent keystore KDF parameter invalid: {msg}"),
            Self::Kdf(e) => write!(f, "agent keystore KDF failed: {e}"),
            Self::Crypto(msg) => write!(f, "agent keystore crypto failed: {msg}"),
            Self::InvalidFormat(msg) => write!(f, "agent keystore format invalid: {msg}"),
            Self::HomeUnavailable => write!(f, "agent keystore default path requires HOME"),
            Self::Bincode(msg) => write!(f, "agent keystore serde failed: {msg}"),
        }
    }
}

impl std::error::Error for AgentKeystoreError {}

impl From<std::io::Error> for AgentKeystoreError {
    fn from(e: std::io::Error) -> Self {
        Self::Io(e)
    }
}

/// TB-9 Atom 2 helper: read the durable keystore password from env (with a
/// hardcoded local-dev fallback, acceptable for solo-runs per
/// `feedback_kolmogorov_compression`). Wraps the result in `SecretString` so
/// callers (binaries) don't need to depend on `secrecy` directly.
pub fn keystore_password_from_env() -> SecretString {
    let raw = env::var("TURINGOS_AGENT_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD")
        .unwrap_or_else(|_| "tb9-local-dev-password-replace-in-production".to_string());
    SecretString::new(raw.into())
}

/// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: resolve `~/.turingos/keystore/agent_keystore.enc`.
///
/// `TURINGOS_AGENT_KEYSTORE_PATH` overrides. Default never points into the
/// repository, CAS, or runtime_repo directories.
pub fn default_agent_keystore_path() -> Result<PathBuf, AgentKeystoreError> {
    if let Ok(path) = env::var("TURINGOS_AGENT_KEYSTORE_PATH") {
        return Ok(PathBuf::from(path));
    }
    let home = env::var("HOME").map_err(|_| AgentKeystoreError::HomeUnavailable)?;
    Ok(PathBuf::from(home)
        .join(".turingos")
        .join("keystore")
        .join("agent_keystore.enc"))
}

/// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: load durable keystore from disk if present, else
/// return an empty map. Returns `(secrets, fresh)` where `fresh=true` if the
/// path did not exist at call time.
pub fn load_or_empty(
    keystore_path: &Path,
    password: &SecretString,
) -> Result<(BTreeMap<String, [u8; SECRET_KEY_LEN]>, bool), AgentKeystoreError> {
    if !keystore_path.exists() {
        return Ok((BTreeMap::new(), true));
    }
    let bytes = fs::read(keystore_path)?;
    let encoded = EncryptedBundle::decode(&bytes)?;
    let mut key = derive_key(password, &encoded.salt, encoded.kdf)?;
    let cipher = ChaCha20Poly1305::new_from_slice(&key)
        .map_err(|_| AgentKeystoreError::Crypto("bad cipher key"))?;
    let plaintext = cipher
        .decrypt(
            Nonce::from_slice(&encoded.nonce),
            encoded.ciphertext.as_ref(),
        )
        .map_err(|_| AgentKeystoreError::Crypto("keystore authentication failed"))?;
    key.zeroize();
    let cfg = bincode::config::standard()
        .with_big_endian()
        .with_fixed_int_encoding();
    let (secrets, consumed): (BTreeMap<String, [u8; SECRET_KEY_LEN]>, usize) =
        bincode::serde::decode_from_slice(&plaintext, cfg)
            .map_err(|e| AgentKeystoreError::Bincode(e.to_string()))?;
    if consumed != plaintext.len() {
        return Err(AgentKeystoreError::Bincode(format!(
            "trailing bytes: consumed {consumed} of {}",
            plaintext.len()
        )));
    }
    Ok((secrets, false))
}

/// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: encrypt + atomic-write the durable keystore.
pub fn save(
    keystore_path: &Path,
    password: &SecretString,
    secrets: &BTreeMap<String, [u8; SECRET_KEY_LEN]>,
) -> Result<(), AgentKeystoreError> {
    let kdf = KdfParams::from_env()?;
    let mut salt = [0u8; SALT_LEN];
    let mut nonce = [0u8; NONCE_LEN];
    getrandom::getrandom(&mut salt).map_err(AgentKeystoreError::Entropy)?;
    getrandom::getrandom(&mut nonce).map_err(AgentKeystoreError::Entropy)?;

    let mut key = derive_key(password, &salt, kdf)?;
    let cipher = ChaCha20Poly1305::new_from_slice(&key)
        .map_err(|_| AgentKeystoreError::Crypto("bad cipher key"))?;
    let cfg = bincode::config::standard()
        .with_big_endian()
        .with_fixed_int_encoding();
    let mut plaintext = bincode::serde::encode_to_vec(secrets, cfg)
        .map_err(|e| AgentKeystoreError::Bincode(e.to_string()))?;
    let ciphertext = cipher
        .encrypt(Nonce::from_slice(&nonce), plaintext.as_ref())
        .map_err(|_| AgentKeystoreError::Crypto("keystore encryption failed"))?;
    plaintext.zeroize();
    key.zeroize();

    let bundle = EncryptedBundle {
        kdf,
        salt,
        nonce,
        ciphertext,
    }
    .encode()?;

    write_keystore_0600_atomic(keystore_path, &bundle)?;
    Ok(())
}

fn derive_key(
    password: &SecretString,
    salt: &[u8; SALT_LEN],
    kdf: KdfParams,
) -> Result<[u8; DERIVED_KEY_LEN], AgentKeystoreError> {
    let params = Params::new(
        kdf.memory_kib,
        kdf.iterations,
        kdf.lanes,
        Some(DERIVED_KEY_LEN),
    )
    .map_err(|err| AgentKeystoreError::KdfParam(err.to_string()))?;
    let argon2 = Argon2::new(Algorithm::Argon2id, Version::V0x13, params);
    let mut key = [0u8; DERIVED_KEY_LEN];
    argon2
        .hash_password_into(password.expose_secret().as_bytes(), salt, &mut key)
        .map_err(AgentKeystoreError::Kdf)?;
    Ok(key)
}

fn write_keystore_0600_atomic(path: &Path, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<(), AgentKeystoreError> {
    if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
        fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
    }
    let tmp = path.with_extension("enc.tmp");
    {
        let mut options = OpenOptions::new();
        options.write(true).create(true).truncate(true);
        set_open_options_mode_0600(&mut options);
        let mut file = options.open(&tmp)?;
        file.write_all(bytes)?;
        file.sync_all()?;
    }
    fs::rename(&tmp, path)?;
    set_file_permissions_0600(path)?;
    Ok(())
}

#[cfg(unix)]
fn set_open_options_mode_0600(options: &mut OpenOptions) {
    use std::os::unix::fs::OpenOptionsExt;
    options.mode(0o600);
}

#[cfg(not(unix))]
fn set_open_options_mode_0600(_options: &mut OpenOptions) {}

#[cfg(unix)]
fn set_file_permissions_0600(path: &Path) -> Result<(), AgentKeystoreError> {
    use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
    fs::set_permissions(path, fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o600)).map_err(AgentKeystoreError::Io)
}

#[cfg(not(unix))]
fn set_file_permissions_0600(_path: &Path) -> Result<(), AgentKeystoreError> {
    Ok(())
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
struct KdfParams {
    memory_kib: u32,
    iterations: u32,

    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: snapshot the public-key map as a manifest object
    /// (sorted by AgentId for determinism).
    pub fn manifest(&self) -> AgentPubkeyManifest {
        AgentPubkeyManifest {
            agents: self
                .keypairs
                .iter()
                .map(|(id, kp)| (id.0.clone(), kp.public_key().to_hex()))
                .collect(),
        }
    }

    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: path to the on-disk manifest.
    pub fn manifest_path(&self) -> &Path {
        &self.manifest_path
    }

    /// Atomic write: tmp file + rename. JSON pretty-printed for inspection.
    /// TB-9 Atom 1: also re-encrypts + atomically writes the durable keystore
    /// when `self.durable` is populated.
    fn persist_manifest(&self) -> Result<(), AgentKeypairError> {
        let manifest = self.manifest();
        let serialized = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&manifest)
            .map_err(|e| AgentKeypairError::Serde(e.to_string()))?;
        let tmp = self.manifest_path.with_extension("json.tmp");
        {
            let mut f = OpenOptions::new()
                .write(true)
                .create(true)
                .truncate(true)
                .open(&tmp)?;
            f.write_all(serialized.as_bytes())?;
            f.sync_all()?;
        }
        std::fs::rename(&tmp, &self.manifest_path)?;

        if let Some(durable) = &self.durable {
            let mut secrets: BTreeMap<String, [u8; AGENT_SECRET_LEN]> = BTreeMap::new();
            for (id, kp) in &self.keypairs {
                secrets.insert(id.0.clone(), kp.secret_bytes());
            }
            crate::runtime::agent_keystore::save(
                &durable.keystore_path,
                &durable.password,
                &secrets,
            )
            .map_err(|e| AgentKeypairError::Serde(format!("durable keystore: {e}")))?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}

// ── Public manifest: deserialized read-side ──────────────────────────────────

/// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: on-disk shape of `agent_pubkeys.json`.
/// `verify_chaintape` (Atom 4) reads this and rebuilds an `AgentPublicKeyMap`
/// to verify each WorkTx signature.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
pub struct AgentPubkeyManifest {
    /// AgentId.0 → AgentPublicKey hex
    pub agents: BTreeMap<String, String>,
}

impl AgentPubkeyManifest {
    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: load and parse the manifest from disk.
    pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, AgentKeypairError> {
        let mut f = OpenOptions::new().read(true).open(path)?;
        let mut buf = Vec::new();
        f.read_to_end(&mut buf)?;
        let manifest: AgentPubkeyManifest = serde_json::from_slice(&buf)
            .map_err(|e| AgentKeypairError::Serde(e.to_string()))?;
        Ok(manifest)
    }

    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: resolve an AgentId to its pinned public key
    /// (None if unknown).
    pub fn get(&self, agent_id: &AgentId) -> Option<AgentPublicKey> {
        self.agents
            .get(&agent_id.0)
            .and_then(|hex| AgentPublicKey::from_hex(hex).ok())
    }
}

// ── Verification (replay-side) ───────────────────────────────────────────────

pub fn verify_agent_signature(
    signature: &AgentSignature,
    digest: &[u8; 32],

    digest: &[u8; 32],

) -> Result<(), AgentKeypairError> {

        .map_err(|e| AgentKeypairError::Verify(format!("from_bytes: {e}")))?;
    let sig = Signature::from_bytes(signature.as_bytes());
    verifying
        .verify(digest, &sig)
        .map_err(|e| AgentKeypairError::Verify(format!("verify: {e}")))
}

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

I.1.1 multi-agent verification. Predecessor TB-N1-A3 R2 hash afde6670 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N1-AGENT-ECONOMY Phase 2 A3 R2 Codex Q4 fix (2026-05-10; session #36; PRE-§8 dual audit R1 Codex CHALLENGE Q4): 3 OMEGA callsites' `u as i64` casts replaced with `i64::try_from(u).unwrap_or(i64::MAX)` saturating cast. Pre-fix `u as i64` would WRAP NEGATIVE if agent submitted `stake_micro > i64::MAX` (≈ 9.2 quintillion μC; theoretically possible via JSON injection though no real agent will), routing to `StakeInsufficient` (Step-4a `<= 0`) instead of the intended `StakeBalanceExceeded` (Step-4b `> balance`). Saturating-to-i64::MAX preserves fail-closed admission: u64 overflow → i64::MAX which always exceeds any agent balance (max realistic 30M μC) → fires StakeBalanceExceeded as Codex Q4 demanded. Pure-defensive fix; no protocol-wire-shape change; no sequencer change. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 hash 5dfd5142 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual external audit R2 closure): Codex R2 OVERALL CHALLENGE/ship-clean (all 4 R1 VETOs closed; only doc-drift residual). Gemini R2 OVERALL VETO on Q1 (architectural enforcement gap) + Q2 (degenerate smoke). Conservative resolution per feedback_dual_audit_conflict (VETO>CHALLENGE>PASS): file OBS_R024 + TB-17 PRE-17.5 prerequisite (sequencer-side Boltzmann enforcement gate, Class 4 surface, deferred per umbrella charter §0 Class 3 envelope respect + project_tb11_to_tb17_roadmap phase boundary). Q2 closed in r4 (BOLTZMANN_SEED=12345 → distribution {iter-0:1, iter-1:2} non-trivial). This commit's source change: stale-comment cleanup ONLY (no functional code change) — evaluator.rs:1241-1250 docblock above the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS env-var check now mentions "no fallback (since .fix r1)" and "non-None Shannon entropy ≥ 0.5" instead of the pre-fix-r1 wording about fallback to produced_worktx_ids.last() and 0.25 threshold. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental hash 346a6a3c superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental (2026-05-05; preseed-settle barrier added BEFORE the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS iter loop): r2 smoke surfaced a race that the per-iter Codex CHALLENGE #2 fix (removing pre-iter await) inadvertently exposed. The preseed phase queues TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED seed via bus.submit_typed_tx but does NOT block on their commits. Without a settle barrier, iter=0's q_snapshot can fall in the middle of the preseed queue → iter-0 WorkTx parent_state_root captured before another preseed tx commits → apply_one rejects iter-0 with StaleParent → iter-0 lands on L4.E → iter-1's snap.price_index empty → v2_pick=None for iter-1 (the r2 smoke shape: tx_kind_counts.work=3 / l4e_count=2 / iter-1 v2_pick=None instead of expected Some(iter-0)). Fix: poll q_snapshot at start of FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS block until state_root is stable for one poll cycle (preseed queue drained); 50 × 200ms = 10s budget; FAIL-CLOSED if barrier doesn't settle (signals wedged queue). This matches the "settle then dispatch" pattern that the original (commit b5118fd) hook accidentally got via the per-iter pre-await — the per-iter pre-await was structurally on the wrong side of submit (Codex CH2) but it ALSO accidentally drained the preseed queue before iter 0's parent_state_root capture. The settle barrier is the principled replacement. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (without barrier) hash fada36b4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual-audit closure of Codex VETO #1..#4 + CHALLENGE #1..#4 + Gemini Q1/Q2/Q5/Q7/Q8/Q12 CHALLENGES on parent commit b5118fd): (V2 Codex VETO #2 — produced_worktx_ids.push moved to AFTER tb8_await_state_root_advance success; bus.submit_typed_tx is async per src/bus.rs:136-138, so push-before-commit could register a queued tx_id that subsequently failed dispatch). (V4 Codex VETO #4 — env-var parse + CAS / ProposalTelemetry / WorkTx-construction / submit / commit-await failures all converted from warn-and-skip to FAIL-CLOSED via std::process::exit(3); for an explicit user-activated arena hook, partial-success silently biasing the entropy distribution is unacceptable). (CH2 Codex CHALLENGE #2 — pre-iteration tb8_await_state_root_advance was placed BEFORE the tx it was supposed to observe (helper contract per src/runtime/adapter.rs:570-576 says "submit, then await POST-submit"); fix removes the pre-await and only retains the post-submit await per iteration so the next iter's snapshot reflects the prior commit). (CH4 Codex CHALLENGE #4 — removed produced_worktx_ids.last() fallback when v2_pick is None; strict semantics now: parent_tx tracks the v2 selector's authoritative pick or None for root, never a manufactured edge from local state). (V4 supplemental — sequencer.q_snapshot Err converted to FAIL-CLOSED exit). Smoke script `run_tb_16_x_2_4_smoke_2026-05-05.sh` companion fixes (V3): set -euo pipefail (was -uo); explicit RC capture via `|| RC=$?` + abort on RC ≠ 0; refuse to overwrite populated PROBLEM_DIR without ALLOW_REUSE=1; audit_tape stdout captured into AT_LOG variable so pipefail propagates the binary RC. .2.3 + .2.5 smoke scripts also receive the set -e + RC capture carry-over (cheap consistency fix). BOLTZMANN_EPSILON_NUM/DEN env vars (default 9/10) injected so the v2 selector's epsilon-greedy uniform-random branch fires 90% of the time on iter 1+ (vs 10% default), producing diverse parent_tx instead of always-iter-0 lex-tiebreak. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4 hash 5a989d15 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.4 — Multi-WorkTx + Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS=<staker>:<count>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional .2.5 bankruptcy-after-accepted seed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects N (≥3) real-signed WorkTxs serially. Each iteration: (1) snapshot bus → call boltzmann_select_parent_v2(snap.price_index, snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut seeded_rng) — the existing TB-14 Atom 5 integer-rational scheduler (sdk/actor.rs:57); (2) parent_tx = v2 pick OR fallback to most recent produced WorkTx tx_id (so iter 1+ has SOME parent; iter 0 is genuinely root); (3) build + write ProposalTelemetry to CAS via build_for_evaluator_append_with_parent(parent_tx) (mandatory for audit assertion id=24 chain integrity per .2.5 r2 lesson); (4) construct real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) referencing the telemetry CID as proposal_cid; (5) submit + await commit; (6) capture tx_id for next iteration's fallback. Closes the missing R3 "Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise" gap: prior chains had at most 1 admitted WorkTx per task, so the v2 selector had no candidate set to choose from. Audit assertion id=43 (boltzmann_parent_selection_diversity, Layer E supplemental, added in .2.5 commit) verifies Shannon entropy ≥ 0.25 across same-task ProposalTelemetry.parent_tx distribution. DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.4 said "verify boltzmann_select_parent_v2 is called in WorkTx admission path; STEP_B-PROTOCOL TRIGGERED". Position taken: STEP_B refers to sequencer-side dispatch path; v2 selector is ALREADY called proposal-side at evaluator.rs:1828 (captured into _v2_canonical_pick, currently unused). For SG-16.x.2.4 (≥3 WorkTx with parent_selection_entropy ≥ 0.5), the parent_tx record lives in ProposalTelemetry (CAS object) — proposal-time data, not sequencer-side admission data. No sequencer.rs touch needed; STEP_B not triggered. Class 3 dual external audit (Codex + Gemini) STILL applied because the surface is high-impact (Boltzmann RUNTIME wire-up is the V3L-14 anti-star-topology mechanism; Art II.2.1 alarm threshold gate). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r3 hash d39c67d1 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 r3 (2026-05-05; smoke r2 surfaced audit assertion id=24 (proposal_telemetry_chain) HALT — the seed WorkTx's proposal_cid was computed via Cid::from_content of a literal string but the bytes were never written to CAS, so cas.get(proposal_cid) returned NotFound, halting the audit. Fix: the seed-WorkTx hook now opens CasStore::open(&bundle.cas_path), constructs a minimal ProposalTelemetry via build_for_evaluator_append (which writes proposal_artifact bytes to CAS as a side-effect), then calls proposal_telemetry::write_to_cas to persist the telemetry itself and uses the returned CID as the WorkTx.proposal_cid. ProposalTelemetry parent_tx is None for the .2.5 seed (single-WorkTx scenario; .2.4 will use Boltzmann pick). verification_result_cid is None (no Lean verification on the seed; id=24 only halts on missing telemetry CAS bytes, NOT on missing VerificationResult). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r2 hash c19dfcbe (which broke audit id=24) superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.5 — AutopsyCapsule real-bankruptcy chain): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED=<staker>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects a real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) so stakes_t has ≥1 entry for `task-{run_id}` at the time TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 emits TaskBankruptcyTx (line ~3150). The TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5 hook (sequencer.rs:1374) then calls derive_autopsies_for_bankruptcy(pre_econ, bk, ...) which iterates stakes_t and emits an AgentAutopsyCapsule with loss_reason_class=Bankruptcy + loss_amount = stake.amount. Closes the missing R2 P4 path "WorkTx-accepted → got accepted → then the task went bankrupt". The seeded WorkTx is REAL (real Ed25519 signature via AgentKeypairRegistry, real predicate_results, real stake debit from preseeded balance) — NOT a synthetic-rejection gate (compare make_synthetic_worktx at line ~1086 which uses synthetic_rejection_for_l4e_gate=true → L4.E only). Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 to materialize WorkTx (admit) → TerminalSummary → TaskBankruptcy → AutopsyCapsule in CAS + AutopsyIndex entry in agent_autopsies_t. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.3 hash e1c4d057 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.3 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.3 — CompleteSetRedeem env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_REDEEM=<owner>:<outcome>:<share_units> env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (parallel to FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE; OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional so it fires on both OMEGA-Confirm success and MaxTxExhausted+FORCE_BANKRUPTCY paths), constructs an agent-signed CompleteSetRedeemTx via make_real_complete_set_redeem_signed_by + submits via bus.submit_typed_tx. Pairs with TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED (mints provider's YES+NO shares) + TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY (transitions market to Bankrupt → NO wins) to produce a single chain containing CompleteSetMint → TaskBankruptcy → CompleteSetRedeem sequence — closes the missing 6th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 11-of-13 → 12-of-13). DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.3 spec'd 4 env-var parts (owner:event_id:outcome:share); implementation uses 3 (owner:outcome:share_units) — event_id auto-derived from `task-{run_id}` because run_id contains a unix-ms timestamp minted at evaluator entry (run_id.rs:21) and is unpredictable from the smoke script. Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's auto-derive pattern (line ~3154). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix hash 12489ab4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix (2026-05-05; FC1-emit hook placement bug-fix on TB-16.x.2.2 commit 5e32cbf): the original 5e32cbf placed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE in the post-bundle cleanup section reachable ONLY on the MaxTxExhausted exit path (line 2895 P0-A comment + 2902 mark_final_accept). As filed, smoke r2 produced tx_kind_counts.challenge_resolve=0 + id=42 SKIPPED + ship-gate false-positive (grep matched the field name "challenge_resolve" with value 0 + dashboard run_id slug). This fix mirrors the FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE block onto BOTH OMEGA-Confirm exit paths (full-proof @ ~line 2253 + per-tactic @ ~line 2755), each with post-emit tb8_await_state_root_advance because the OMEGA exit drops bundle without bundle.shutdown drain (evaluator.rs:2936-2938). Original commit's claim "two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive" remains accurate — but the OMEGA path had NO cleanup section at all. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash 993452e4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER on the same arena profile to produce a single chain containing Challenge → ChallengeResolve parent-child relationship — closes the missing 5th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 10-of-13 → 11-of-13). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.1 hash eb718a23 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.1 (2026-05-04; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.1 — TaskExpire env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_EXPIRE=1 env-var: after MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule write + (optional) FORCE_BANKRUPTCY emit, calls tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible(seq, current_logical_t=next_logical_t_peek(), expiry_delta_logical_t=0) so every Open/Bankrupt task with elapsed > 0 emits a TaskExpireTx (ExpireReason::Deadline for Open; ExpireReason::BankruptcyTriggered when FORCE_BANKRUPTCY also fired). Predecessor TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 hash 729a4c5e superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 (2026-05-04; architect §7.3 FR-16.3 + FR-16.4 + FR-16.7): ADDITIVE arena driver hooks. (1) TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER=<agent_id> env-var: after VerifyTx OMEGA-Confirm and before FinalizeReward emit, the named agent submits a real-signed ChallengeTx targeting the WorkTx (FR-16.3). (2) TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED=<provider>:<amount_micro> env-var: after preseed EscrowLock, the named provider submits MarketSeedTx + CompleteSetMintTx against the task's EventId (FR-16.4). All three are env-var-gated; no behavior change to existing user-task / preseed paths. Predecessor TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up hash 3530a2d5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up (2026-05-03; architect ruling step 1 — Codex R1 primary VETO closure): the canonical TxId returned by `boltzmann_select_parent_v2` MUST NOT flow into legacy shadow `bus.append` parent_id (kernel.tape uses a different (shadow) id namespace; canonical TxId becomes a dangling citation → kernel.append rejects). Per architect ruling: "Use None unless a real shadow id exists." No canonical → shadow id mapping is currently available; pass None (legacy default). The v2 selector still runs (its result captured as `_v2_canonical_pick` for observability + future canonical wire-up — see B′ step 4 CanonicalNodeGraph + parent_tx replacement for last_tx_by_agent), but its output is explicitly NOT passed to bus.append. Closes Codex R1 primary VETO defect #1.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 (2026-05-03; closing OBS_TB_12_LEGACY_CPMM_QUARANTINE): production wire-swap. (1) Imports updated: `BoltzmannParams + boltzmann_select_parent` (legacy f64) → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2 + BoltzmannMaskPolicy` (integer-rational). (2) `BusConfig` literals (×2) drop `system_lp_amount` field. (3) `let params = BoltzmannParams::from_env()` → `let policy = BoltzmannMaskPolicy::from_env()`. (4) Tick-time logging derives `market_count` + top-N price ticker from `bus.snapshot().price_index` (cross-multiplication argmax; rendered `n/d`, never decimal). (5) Per-tx prompt build derives `market_ticker_str` from `snap.price_index` + queries `prompt_balance` from `bus.sequencer.q_snapshot().economic_state_t.balances_t` (replaces legacy `snap.get_balance`; the `f64` is the `prompt.rs` render contract — `prompt.rs` is not a TB-14 module surface). (6) Boltzmann selector call replaced: `boltzmann_select_parent(&snap.tape, &prices, &params, &mut rng)` → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2(&snap.price_index, &snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut rng).map(|tx| tx.0)`. Predicate-blind (CR-14.1 + halt-trigger #1) — purely a scheduling priority pick. Predecessor TB-12 Atom 0.5(a) hash 8f1b97f1 superseded. (2026-05-03; architect 2026-05-03 ruling §1.1 + §8 Atom 0.5; TB-11 G3/G4 carry-forward closure): added 4 EvidenceCapsule counters (tb11_lean_error_count / tb11_sorry_block_count / tb11_protocol_parse_failure_count / tb11_partial_accept_count) instrumented at existing classify_lean_error / classify_parse_error / step_partial_ok call sites; sorry-block vs lean-error split via err_detail.contains("sorry") || err_detail.contains("forbidden_payload"); MAX_TX exhausted exit point (inside `if let Some(bundle) = chaintape_bundle`, BEFORE bundle.shutdown) now writes EvidenceCapsule to CAS via runtime::evidence_capsule::write_evidence_capsule + emits TerminalSummary on-chain via runtime::adapter::tb11_emit_terminal_summary_for_run with capsule_cid; bundle.shutdown drains the queued TerminalSummary tx via apply_one. Mirror of TB-8 tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify pattern. Predecessor TB-10 Atom 1+3 hash a5b30cd1 superseded.

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:1367:218:"src/runtime/mod.rs" = "f72bbda70756958c83f5873b3b75585196e7ce821f82e0ca65de32f60f244875"  # rehashed by TB-G G1.1 (2026-05-11 session #40; Class-4 STEP_B; architect §8 SIGNED "好，确认可以 ship" 2026-05-11 session #39 close — canonical Class-4 §8 form; packet §2): added `RuntimeChaintapeConfig.resume_existing_chain: bool` field + strict env gate `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME == "1"` in `from_env` (matches!(Ok("1")) — no truthy-string footgun per packet §5 Q4) + resume branch in `build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q` (when `resume_existing_chain && head_commit_oid().is_some()`: invokes new private helper `bootstrap_resume_state` which reads `pinned_pubkeys.json` + `initial_q_state.json` fail-closed if missing, replays every L4 entry via the canonical `replay_full_transition` FC2 Boot primitive shared with `verify_chaintape`, generates a NEW keypair for a NEW epoch `max_existing_epoch + 1`, appends to manifest so prior-epoch entries still verify, returns `(new_keypair, new_epoch, pinned, replayed_q, chain_length)`) + `Sequencer::new_at_logical_t(seed_q, chain_length)` call so the strict `len + 1` invariant holds on the next commit + new private helper `decode_pubkey_hex_32`. Default `resume_existing_chain: false` preserves the original TB-6 `BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo` fail-closed gate (SG-G1.4 back-compat regression). 5 SG-G1.* tests in `tests/constitution_g1_resume.rs` cover the gates. Constitutional anchor: FC2-Boot §3.2 "every real evidence run must be replayable from genesis_report + ChainTape + CAS + agent registry + system pubkeys" + §4.1 G-009 Path C "replay reconstructs HEAD_t" — resume is the boot-time instance of that replay. Packet §2 adjacent-surfaces row described `head_t_witness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` as the QState-rebuild primitive, but in code that helper only reconstructs the 6-field HeadTWitness derived view from L4/L4.E/CAS ref OIDs (Stage A3 SG-A3.4 derived-view boundary); per FC2 §3.2 + §4.1 the canonical QState replay primitive is `replay_full_transition`, which is what the resume path uses (user 2026-05-11 directive: "关于内核一定要对齐宪法和宪法中的三个flowchart"). handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:1371:222:"src/runtime/adapter.rs" = "a84afccf67963ae22788f111d23332221d0a7ed6b65a89a96b5e32c68b7aa5d4"  # rehashed by TB-N3 A3 (2026-05-11; Class-4 STEP_B; architect ruling 2026-05-11 amendments 3-6 + Q1+Q2+Q6): added 2 NEW pub items — `NodeMarketEmitOutcome` enum (5 variants: BudgetExhausted / AlreadyExists / Created{event_id, pool_seed_micro} / SubmitFailed{step, message} / PollTimeout{step}) + `tb_n3_emit_node_market_after_work_accept` async helper (mirrors tb_n2_emit_event_resolve_after_finalize poll-then-emit pattern). Behavior: snapshot Q → idempotency check (cpmm_pools_t[event_id] present → AlreadyExists) → MarketMakerBudget balance precheck (< seed_micro → BudgetExhausted; fail-closed per architect §3.4 + §7 forbidden "ghost liquidity") → sign+submit TaskOpenTx(sponsor=MarketMakerBudget, task=node_survive:work_tx_id) → tb8_await_state_root_advance → sign+submit MarketSeedTx(provider=MarketMakerBudget, event_id, collateral=seed_micro) → await → sign+submit CpmmPoolTx(provider=MarketMakerBudget, event_id, seed_yes=seed_no=seed_micro as u128) → await → Created{event_id, pool_seed_micro}. event_id constructed via node_survive_event_id (architect amendment 1). Architect amendment 4: caller MUST invoke ONLY after WorkTx L4-accepted (no L4.E auto-pool). Architect amendment 3: all 3 sub-tx route via canonical Sequencer::submit_agent_tx admission — no emit_system_tx bypass; no hand-written treasury debit / pool reserve mutation. Best-effort contract: failures return typed NodeMarketEmitOutcome variant; Err reserved for EmitSystemError (currently unreachable since this helper does not touch emit_system_tx — kept for symmetry with sibling helpers). Predecessor TB-N3 A2 hash 2a2f3866 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N3 A2 + A3 prep (2026-05-11; architect ruling 2026-05-11 amendments 1+3+Q5): added 3 NEW pub items — `InvestRouteError` enum (7 variants: ZeroAmount / NegativeAmount / AmountExceedsBalance / MalformedNode / UnknownEvent / PoolNotActive / KeypairError) with `to_no_trade_reason()` mapping into the §8.2 taxonomy + `public_summary()` for the trace.reason_summary_public bound; `tb_n3_invest_to_router_tx` (sync constructor; pre-classifies via optional snapshot then signs canonical BuyWithCoinRouterSigningPayload via AgentKeypairRegistry::sign; mirrors make_real_*_signed_by shape; min_out_shares = 0 per architect Q5 MVP; event_id constructed via node_survive_event_id per amendment 1); `make_real_cpmm_pool_signed_by` (sync; mirrors make_real_market_seed_signed_by shape; symmetric init; required by forthcoming A3 emit helper for canonical signing path per amendment 3 "A3 应优先用 MarketSeedTx ... 不得手写 treasury debit / pool reserve"). Predecessor TB-N2 B2 R2 hash 005e1efb superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N2 B2 R2 race fix (2026-05-11; Codex G2 R1 Q8 VETO closure): `tb_n2_emit_event_resolve_after_finalize` now accepts `verify_tx_id: &TxId` 3rd param and derives `claim_id = TxId(format!("claim-{verify_tx_id}"))` (mirrors tb8 helper's claim_id_inner at adapter.rs:622). Poll loop now requires BOTH `claims_t[claim_id].status == ClaimStatus::Finalized` AND `task_markets_t[task_id].state == Open` before emit. The claim Finalized witness signals FinalizeReward dispatch arm has applied (state_root advanced past R_0), so the subsequent `emit_system_tx` captures the post-FinalizeReward state_root → no StaleParent L4.E race. Prior R1 helper polled only task_markets_t (which is unchanged by FinalizeReward); smoke evidence `handover/evidence/stage_b3_smoke_b2_20260511T012401Z/deepseek-v4-flash/seed1/rep1/P002_aime_1983_p2/runtime_repo/rejections.jsonl:9` showed `tx_kind:EventResolve public_summary:stale_parent_root` — the B2 transition was rejected at apply time, leaving task_markets_t.state unchanged at Open and the CPMM lifecycle gap §3.3 not actually closed. R2 fix is binding-tested by `tests/constitution_n2_event_resolve.rs::sg_n2_b2_9_adapter_polls_claim_finalized_before_emit_r2`. Predecessor TB-N2 B2 R1 hash 18bc199e superseded.  # rehashed by TB-N2 B2 (2026-05-11): added `tb_n2_emit_event_resolve_after_finalize` poll-then-emit helper (mirrors `tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify` pattern). Polls `q_snapshot` until task_markets_t[task_id].state == Open; emits `SystemEmitCommand::EventResolve` via `Sequencer::emit_system_tx`. Best-effort contract: Ok(true) on emit / Ok(false) on poll-budget-expiry or task already resolved / Err on unexpected emit_system_tx error. Closes the CPMM lifecycle automation gap from the OMEGA-Confirm exit path. Predecessor hash 48da399a superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.3 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.3 — CompleteSetRedeem env-var trigger): ADDITIVE — added make_real_complete_set_redeem_signed_by(keypairs, parent_state_root, event_task, owner, outcome, share_units, suffix, timestamp_logical) helper. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 hash c1360a73 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 (2026-05-05; doc-only follow-up addressing Codex CHALLENGE E.3 from the .fix Class 3 audit): the prior .fix Patch E docstring referenced a nonexistent `Sequencer::set_current_round_for_test` symbol while explaining the time-unit difference between this helper and tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible. NO behavior change — pure docstring edit; helper signature, eligibility logic, and call surface are unchanged from .fix Patch E. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E hash 3e770c4b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E (2026-05-05; tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible eligibility-boundary semantic-fix): the original TB-16.x.2.2 helper used `if elapsed <= window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` — i.e. require elapsed > delta. With delta=0 the helper required ≥1 round to pass, contradicting its own docstring claim "delta=0 makes every Open case immediately eligible". Smoke r3 (post-Patch-A) showed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE batch returning count=0 because q.q_t.current_round is NOT auto-advanced per-tx (unlike tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible's caller-supplied current_logical_t which auto-advances per-tx); the OMEGA-Confirm path opens the case and scans within the same round, so elapsed=0 for delta=0 was always skipped. Patch E flips the boundary to `if elapsed < window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` (require elapsed >= delta), aligning impl with docstring. Sole callers of this helper today are the FORCE_-prefixed arena hooks; no production caller depends on the prior off-by-one boundary. Docstring updated to reflect `>= delta` and to clarify the time-unit difference vs tb11. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash a9bbb1ac superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE — added tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible(seq, window_delta_logical_t, default_resolution) helper. Mirrors tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible shape: scan economic_state_t.challenge_cases_t for entries with status=Open AND q.q_t.current_round - case.opened_at_round > window_delta; emit ChallengeResolveTx via emit_system_tx (SystemEmitCommand::ChallengeResolve) for each eligible case. Returns (count, bonds_released_micro). emit_system_tx for ChallengeResolve does NOT pre-check case existence/status (sequencer.rs:2590 — construction is unconditional); dispatch-time AlreadyResolved/ChallengeNotFound surfaces as L4.E rejection, not Err here. Mirror existing make_real_worktx_signed_by / make_real_verifytx_signed_by patterns. No behavior change to existing helpers. Predecessor hash 9f0e3c994fbcff07eedf6a9020db9e9c76b6b589b075e78569c4e38fb2fa4e0d superseded.  # rehashed by Stage C P-M5 / Phase F.4 (2026-05-09 session #32; re-apply Class-3 per remediation directive §1.C row 4 + architect manual §7.6 verbatim Buy YES with NO / Buy NO with YES formulas): added DOMAIN_AGENT_CPMM_SWAP constant + SwapDirection enum (BuyYesWithNo / BuyNoWithYes; #[repr(u8)]) + CpmmSwapTx 8-field wire struct (NO timestamp_logical — defect 3 prevention; event_id NOT event_id_kind — defect 4 prevention) + CpmmSwapSigningPayload 7-field signing projection + canonical_digest impl + to_signing_payload projection + TypedTx::CpmmSwap variant + tx_kind dispatch + HasSubmitter (trader as signer) + 5 new TransitionError variants (SwapZeroInput / PoolNotActive / InsufficientSharesForSwap / SwapInsufficientPoolOutput / SwapSlippageExceeded) + their Display arms. NO E.1 binding (Class-3; architect §7.6 specifies behavior + tests, not state struct). Predecessor hash 17d62573333356584d8e622fc6a13e8367a56907a83fde7c8fbc5e3c776a6366 superseded.  # rehashed by Stage C P-M4 / Phase F.3 (2026-05-09 session #31; rebuild post Stage C VETO per remediation directive §1.C row 3): added CpmmPoolTx (architect §7.5 STATE struct verbatim; tx schema implementation-defined as strict 7-field shape mirroring CompleteSetMergeTx P-M2 minimal pattern — NO timestamp_logical drift; defect 4 prevention `event_id` NOT `event_id_kind`) + CpmmPoolSigningPayload (6-field signing projection; F-DEFERRAL-2 sibling binding closure per remediation directive §9) + DOMAIN_AGENT_CPMM_POOL constant + TypedTx::CpmmPool variant + TxKind::CpmmPool dispatch + HasSubmitter impl + 4 new TransitionError variants (InvalidPoolSeed / UnbalancedPoolSeed / InsufficientSharesForPool / PoolAlreadyExists) + their Display arms. Predecessor hash d049337b77 superseded.  # rehashed by Stage C P-M2 / Phase F.1 (2026-05-09 session #29; rebuild post Stage C VETO per remediation directive §1.C row 1): added CompleteSetMergeTx (architect §7.3 verbatim STRICT 6-field — NO timestamp_logical drift; Codex G2 2026-05-09 defect 3 prevention) + CompleteSetMergeSigningPayload (5-field signing projection; F-DEFERRAL-2 closure per remediation directive §9) + DOMAIN_AGENT_COMPLETE_SET_MERGE constant + TypedTx::CompleteSetMerge variant + TxKind::CompleteSetMerge dispatch + HasSubmitter impl + InsufficientSharesForMerge TransitionError variant + Display arm. E.1 binding for P-M2 + P-M2 SigningPayload flipped to LandingStatus::Landed; strict (name, type) pair equality enforced. Predecessor hash 213251db40 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18 Atom A (2026-05-05; architect ruling §3 Atom A + OBS_M0 §5.1): added `RunOutcome::DegradedLLM = 5` + `ExhaustionReason::DegradedLLM = 5` (Art.IV halt-reason taxonomy now 6-way at chain-level / 6-way at capsule-level) + 1:1 projection match arm in `to_run_outcome()` (DegradedLLM -> RunOutcome::DegradedLLM, NOT folded into ProtocolCollapse/SolverGiveUp -> ErrorHalt because architect §2.5 requires DegradedLLM distinguishable for failure-mode coverage in atom H benchmark report). DegradedLLM halt path in evaluator.rs run_swarm fires when N consecutive LLM responses each have completion_tokens < token_floor_threshold (closes OBS_M0_DEEPSEEK_DRIFT §3 silent-hang). FR-18.3: DegradedLLM emits EvidenceCapsule + TerminalSummary via canonical Atom E propagation pipeline; not an evidence-skip backdoor. CR-15.3 — opaque rule identifier; autopsy never depends on live registry. Predecessor TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 hash 44098978 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 (2026-05-03; Codex RQ5 remediation): drop ResolutionRef wrapper struct (incl. resolution_tx_id + claimed_outcome) from CompleteSetRedeemTx + CompleteSetRedeemSigningPayload. Both fields were dead — resolution_tx_id was opaque/L4-unvalidated (per round-4 doc-fix), claimed_outcome was a redundant copy of redeem.outcome used only for an inner-consistency check that was itself dead defense-in-depth (signature covers both; tampering breaks signature). Net: CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; signing payload 8→7 fields. Inner-consistency check removed from sequencer (Step 1); state-mismatch path preserved via existing match arm. InvalidResolutionRef variant + Display preserved (now reads "outcome does not match state"). Wire-format break — no production rows yet. Predecessor TB-12 Atom 1 hash 11e526a1 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-12 Atom 1 (2026-05-03; architect 2026-05-03 ruling §3 + §8 Atom 1): +PositionSide enum +PositionKind enum +NodePosition struct (immutable exposure record per architect §10; NOT Coin holding per CR-12.1). FORBIDDEN: MarketBuy/MarketSell PositionKind variants (TB-13+ trading layer). Predecessor TB-8 Atom 3 hash 26b01d8b superseded.

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:4757:./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3505:`handover/markov_capsules/LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` is an Art. 0.2 平行账本 (parallel ledger) — a global filesystem pointer whose target cid's bytes live in exactly ONE per-problem CAS (TB-16 R3 Round 2 P8_completeset_b's). It carries cross-chain provenance with no 守恒 test, no derivability from any tape, no architectural anchoring. **Pre-TB-16.x.1 silently masked this with `.ok()` collapse → false-PROCEED Skipped.** TB-16.x.1's fix correctly fail-closes the masking. The remaining Art. 0.2 violation is the **existence of the global pointer itself**, not anything sub-atom 2.x evidence could fix locally.

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:4801:./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:4396:"TB-13 introduces the Polymarket / CTF mathematical core (`1 locked Coin = 1 YES_E + 1 NO_E`) as a non-trading collateral + share accounting layer on top of TB-12's NodePositionsIndex substrate. CompleteSetMintTx is balance↔collateral migration with equal YES/NO claim issuance; CompleteSetRedeemTx redeems winning side post-system-resolved outcome (canonical `task_markets_t.state`); MarketSeedTx provider explicit-funds protocol-owned share inventory. Six-holding CTF (balances + escrows + stakes + challenge_cases + conditional_collateral) preserved bit-equal across all 3 typed-tx; conditional shares are claims, NOT Coin (CR-13.3 + SG-13.2). MIN-semantics `assert_complete_set_balanced` invariant called live from each dispatch arm post-mutation. Submit-time + replay-time agent signature verification (Class 3 admission control) for all 3 variants. Forward-fence (3-layer marker + type-name + hard-import discipline) prevents legacy f64 CPMM contamination. Two architect-spec deviations (`u128 ShareAmount` + `ResolutionRef` removed) endorsed by clean-room auditor as tighter-than-spec but requiring architect ratification before TB-14."
