handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:4811:./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:5219:**Session summary**: Closed the **durable identity** prerequisite per architect directive 2026-05-02 Part C line 1574 ruling 13 ("NodeMarket starts after durable identity AND Lean Proof Task Market MVP"). Run-local Ed25519 keypair lifecycle (TB-7) is now persistent: secrets live in an encrypted-at-rest keystore at `~/.turingos/keystore/agent_keystore.enc` (Argon2id KDF + ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD); the same `Agent_0 → AgentPublicKey` binding survives evaluator restart with a fresh `runtime_repo`. Concurrently, `WalletTool` collapsed to a **read-only projection** of `EconomicState.balances_t` — the parallel f64 ledger and the bus.rs legacy v3 simulation paths (`debit_wallet/credit_wallet/InvestOnly/founder_grant/settle_portfolios/Hayek bounty`) are deleted. **Architect mandate satisfied 5/5**: agent durable key registry ✓, wallet read-only projection ✓, EconomicState canonical ✓, no f64 mutation ✓, cross-run identity ✓. Class 3 risk handled via **recursive self-audit** (4-clause structure: Constitutional / Replay-deterministic / Conservation / User-minimum-contract — all PASS) per `feedback_dual_audit` hybrid-by-risk-class (kernel surface is purely additive — NO new typed_tx variant, NO dispatch arm, NO QState field; external Codex+Gemini deferred post-ship per recursive-audit §8 reasoning). Cross-run Agent_0 pubkey identity empirically verified by `diff -q` over two evaluator runs each with a fresh runtime_repo. TB-9 ship-gate test count: `cargo test --workspace = 723 / 0 / 150` (-2 net vs TB-8 ship 725 baseline; +14 new TB-9 tests, -16 deleted obsolete v3-simulation/f64-mutator tests).

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5053:./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5054:./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5055:./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:25:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5056:./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:26:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5057:./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:27:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5188:./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-1_recharter_2026-04-29.md:72:**WalletTool framing (sharpened post-audit 2026-04-29 CF-3)**: `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is **NOT a legacy mutable adapter** — it is a *read-only projection* of `QState.economic_state_t.balances_t`. Mutations to economic state happen exclusively through the canonical RSP path (`SettlementEngine` / `EscrowVault` / `StakeManager` / `monetary_invariant`). No new RSP code may depend on `WalletTool.credit()` or on `WalletTool` mutating its `HashMap<String, f64>` to represent canonical balance state. Existing `WalletTool` tests stay temporarily as legacy behavior tests; they get removed or rewritten as RSP-1/RSP-2 lands.

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5462:./handover/alignment/STRICT_AUDIT_TBC0_TAPE_2026-05-07.md:194:The round-4 closure report claimed "Constitution Landing Gate ready for §8 sign-off; 21/25 GREEN". Strict audit says: **the empirical tape evidence supports 17/25 chain-resident GREEN, 7 AMBER (3 of which are bug-blocked, not structural-only), and 1 RED**. The TB-C0 work is REAL — the tests exist, the harness ran, the chain is well-formed and replayable, the economy invariants hold, the predicate routing is correct, the no-global-pointer is enforced. But the closure-candidate claim was over-rosy because:
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5471:./handover/alignment/OBS_R022_GLOBAL_LATEST_MARKOV_PARALLEL_LEDGER_2026-05-04.md:48:2. **Hidden source-of-truth**: pre-TB-16.x.1, `read_markov_capsule(...).ok()` collapsed all errors to `None`, masking pointer-CAS mismatches as silent `Skipped`. Round 2 verdict.json shows `markov_constitution_hash_matches: Skipped` — that wasn't legitimate "no prior markov"; it was the masked failure of this very pointer-bytes mismatch. CR-15.6 ("Markov default prevents context poisoning") was bypassed because the pointer's existence was *de facto* the source of truth, not the capsule bytes.

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5570:./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-7_charter_2026-05-01.md:20:Every meaningful LLM proposal in `evaluator::run_swarm` (append branch + complete/OMEGA branch) routes through `bus.submit_typed_tx` as the **AUTHORITATIVE** state-mutation path (legacy `bus.append` either removed, projected from ChainTape, or shadow-only) and lands in L4 (accepted) or L4.E (rejected) on the production chaintape, with `ChainDerivedRunFacts` (NOT full PputResult) bit-exact-equal to the evaluator's structural facts on a defined field set. Frame B closure is achieved iff Gates 1–7 (§8) are GREEN.
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5582:./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-16.x.2_charter_2026-05-04.md:16:| `kill_criteria_tested` | (a) total_supply_micro conservation across all 4 new system-emitted tx kinds; (b) sandbox-prefix invariant (Layer A id=3) honored — no production agent ids leak into emitted txs; (c) replay byte-identity preserved on every sub-atom's evidence chain; (d) no f64 introduced in any new emit path |
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5584:./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-16.x.2_charter_2026-05-04.md:19:| `forbidden` | (a) no f64 in economic/refund paths; (b) no L4 vs L4.E confusion (rejected emits go L4.E with `submit_id`, accepted emits go L4 with `state_root_t` advance); (c) no retroactive evidence rewrite (per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`); (d) no agent-submitted system tx (system-emitted means `emit_system_tx` only); (e) no AMM/CPMM/price-as-truth introduction; (f) no `prediction_market.rs` import (excised in TB-14 Atom 6); (g) no removing existing 38+3 supplemental audit assertions; (h) no agent_id pattern outside `sandbox_prefix` (Layer A id=3 + id=41) |
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5585:./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-16.x.2_charter_2026-05-04.md:20:| `halt_triggers` | HALT if: (i) any sub-atom changes total_supply_micro on accepted-chain; (ii) any sub-atom breaks replay byte-identity; (iii) any sub-atom introduces f64 in economic path; (iv) any sub-atom causes a fence-mechanism doom loop (per `feedback_audit_loop_roi_flip`); (v) any sub-atom skips the smoke evidence requirement (per `feedback_smoke_before_batch`); (vi) `cargo test --workspace` regresses below 907 pass |

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5592:./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-9_charter_2026-05-02.md:48:Concurrently, the runtime carries a parallel f64 ledger in `WalletTool` (`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs:24`) that pre-dates `EconomicState.balances_t` (introduced CO P2.0). Every dispatch arm since TB-3 has mutated `EconomicState.balances_t` (MicroCoin); the legacy `WalletTool.balances` (f64) is mutated only by `bus.rs` market shares code (the v3 simulation path that ChainTape does not touch). **Two ledgers, one canonical chain** — this is the literal definition of `feedback_proxy_running_vs_source_drift`-style trap waiting for the next reviewer to step on it.

There is no f64 ↔ MicroCoin transfer step.

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5674:./handover/tracer_bullets/TB-8_charter_2026-05-02.md:349:If TB-8 ships clean, the highest-value next slot is **TB-9 = Durable AgentRegistry + Wallet Projection**. TB-7R + TB-8 leave the system with run-local agent identity; TB-9 makes it persistent. Without durable identity, future NodeMarket position ownership and TB-10 user-facing payout addresses are not stable. Alongside the durable registry, `WalletTool` collapses to a read-only projection (no f64 mutation; `EconomicState` remains canonical).

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5949:./handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:24:TB-6	shipped	2026-05-01	2026-05-01	P2 (primary; P1/P3 carry-forward)	P1:5,6,7,8,9 P2:6 P3:carry-forward (production-path re-discharge of TB-2..TB-5 invariants under on-disk ChainTape; no new exit criterion)	P1:1,2,3,4 P3:1,2,3 (kill criteria re-tested through production wire-up; P3:9 slash NOT tested — RSP-3.2 territory deferred TB-9)	[660/0/150 PASS @ 17c5e73 across 51 suites; +43 net TB-6 tests across Atoms 1-6 vs TB-5 ship 617/617 baseline] Atom 1 (production runtime bootstrap; STEP_B Phase-0 v1→v2→v2.1 preflight + Codex round-1+2 closed): src/runtime/{mod.rs, adapter.rs} factory + driver wrapper + L4.E JSONL persistence backend + 15 tests. Atom 4 (verify_chaintape replay verifier): src/runtime/verify.rs library + src/bin/verify_chaintape.rs CLI + I90/I90b/I90c integration tests; replay_report.json on smoke evidence shows all 7 architect-mandated boolean indicators true (l4_entries, l4e_entries, ledger_root_verified, system_signatures_verified, state_reconstructed, economic_state_reconstructed, cas_payloads_retrievable). Atom 5 (agent audit trail): src/runtime/agent_audit_trail.rs with AgentProposalRecord (9 fields per architect spec) + AcceptedOrRejected enum + CAS storage + AgentAuditTrailIndex JSONL with prev_hash→hash chain + I91/I91b/I91d (I91d structural witness blocks future schema migrations from adding chain_of_thought / model_deliberation / tool_transcript / raw_prompt / raw_completion / internal_reasoning fields). Atom 6 (RunSummary): src/runtime/run_summary.rs aggregator + src/bin/gen_run_summary.rs CLI + I92/I92b/I92c; run_summary.json on smoke evidence shows 1 accepted tx_id + 1 rejected tx_id + 2 candidate proposal CIDs. Self-audit: handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_6_2026-05-01.md (7/7 architect D1-D7 + 7/7 charter § 4 decision blocks + 20/20 charter § 6 forbidden lines + 3/3 § 8 ship proofs all GREEN); audit label `degraded` (Gemini strategic-tier MODEL_CAPACITY_EXHAUSTED per TB-5 supplement precedent; Codex round-1+2 applied at Atom 1 STEP_B preflight; final Codex impl audit on full TB-6 diff recommended as TB-7 follow-up but non-blocking per charter § 9 + ruling D3 hybrid-by-risk). 5-TB ChainTape production debt CLOSED. Production claim adds: "TuringOS production binary drives the runtime kernel through Sequencer::apply_one to on-disk ChainTape (refs/transitions/main commit chain + cas/ + rejections.jsonl chain-hashed). Replay verifier reconstructs QState + EconomicState from L4 alone via replay_full_transition (CO1.7-impl A4 I-DETHASH witness). Agent audit trail records what the Agent saw + submitted + how the system judged, NOT chain-of-thought (structurally enforced by JSON-grep witness). RunSummary aggregates proposal-level fork visibility. The kernel claims TB-1..TB-5 made are now verifiable from on-disk artifacts produced by an LLM-driven run, not only from cargo test --workspace in-memory state."	?→? handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:5952:./handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:30:# 2026-05-02 TB-9 SHIPPED — "P2 primary + P3 carry-forward: Durable AgentRegistry + Wallet Projection (architect-minimum scope per directive 2026-05-02 Part C line 1574)". Class 3 (auth-crypto-money) recursive self-audit PASS — 4/4 clauses GREEN (Constitutional / Replay-determinism / Conservation / User-minimum-contract); 11/11 ship gates GREEN; 5/5 architect mandates satisfied (agent durable key registry / wallet read-only projection / EconomicState canonical / no f64 mutation / cross-run identity). External dual audit DEFERRED post-ship per recursive-audit §8 — TB-9 kernel surface is purely additive (no typed_tx variant / no dispatch arm / no QState field / no economic mutator); the architect minimum spec leaves zero ambiguity for external auditors to opine on. cargo test --workspace = 723/0/150 (-2 net vs TB-8 ship 725 — added +14 new TB-9 tests across 3 modules; deleted 1 obsolete legacy v3-simulation test file `tests/reward_pull_conservation.rs` with 5 tests + 11 obsolete wallet f64-mutator tests). Smoke evidence: handover/evidence/tb_9_durable_identity_smoke_2026-05-02/ — 3 runs across 2 distinct heldout-49 problems (mathd_algebra_171 fresh-keystore + load-keystore + mathd_algebra_107 regression); 3/3 SOLVED with FinalizeReward + Finalized claim + payout_micro=100,000; cross-run Agent_0 pubkey IDENTICAL (dec9e321e16d39d07353f7a56c5f8e4598ef78c0e79e6886dc5ea382047b6468) across evaluator restart with FRESH runtime_repo each run (architect mandate "agent identity survives run restart" empirically witnessed via `diff -q agent_pubkeys_for_witness.json`); per-run replay_report.json all 7 indicators GREEN; runtime_repo.tar.gz + cas.tar.gz self-contained (TB-8 round-2 RQ3 packaging pattern carried forward). Architectural deltas: (a) NEW src/runtime/agent_keystore.rs (~390 lines) — Argon2id KDF + ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD encryption-at-rest; format magic TOS4AGTKEY1 distinguishes from system_keypair `TOS4SYSKEY1`; default path ~/.turingos/keystore/agent_keystore.enc with TURINGOS_AGENT_KEYSTORE_PATH env override + TURINGOS_AGENT_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD password env; atomic write 0600 mirroring write_keystore_0600 system precedent. (b) EXTEND src/runtime/agent_keypairs.rs — AgentKeypair::from_secret_bytes constructor + secret_bytes accessor + DurableConfig field on registry + AgentKeypairRegistry::generate_or_load_durable load-or-generate factory + persist_manifest now also re-encrypts durable keystore on every new keypair generation; existing TB-7 fail-closed-on-existing semantics retained for `::open(...)` path (tests + non-evaluator callers unchanged). (c) COLLAPSE src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs — DELETE balances HashMap + portfolios HashMap + genesis_done + genesis_coins fields + deduct/credit/record_shares/ensure_agents/save_to_disk/load_from_disk methods; ADD balance(&AgentId, &EconomicState) → MicroCoin projection; on_init no-op + on_pre_append → Pass + query_state → None unconditionally. (d) DELETE src/bus.rs legacy v3 simulation paths — InvestOnly routing block + founder_grant TAPE_ECONOMY_V2 path + settle_portfolios + Hayek bounty payout HAYEK_BOUNTY path + debit_wallet + credit_wallet helpers (~92 lines); InvestOnly signal now → Veto("invest_disabled_tb9"). User invocation '如果6轮audit都不过，要停下来认真思考，根因在哪里' triggered ROI-flip stop at round-7 (commit e66f3bf). cargo test --workspace = 794/0/150 (+35 net vs TB-12 ship 759). Test deltas: tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs (3 architect ship gates SG-13.0.1..3 + 4 internal helper unit tests; two-tier scope split: effective_fence_scope Layer 1 hard-import + effective_layer_2_scope discovered-only); tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs (13 SG-13.x architect-EXACT integration tests + halting-trigger guards + 3-path agent-sig submit-time test); tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs (RQ3 closure: bootstraps Git2LedgerWriter-backed sequencer + real AgentKeypair via AgentKeypairRegistry + manual_replay_from_disk for direct map-equality assertion; evidence at handover/evidence/tb_13_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-03/). REHASH genesis_payload.toml trust_root for src/state/sequencer.rs + src/state/typed_tx.rs + src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs (round-5 cycle). Halting triggers (architect §4.8) NONE triggered: total_supply_micro mutation correct (assert_total_ctf_conserved with empty exempt list called from each arm post-mutation); shares not counted as Coin (sg_13_2 PASS); MarketSeed without balance debit rejected (sg_13_3 PASS); legacy CPMM imports forbidden (3-layer fence); f64 in money path forbidden (SG-13.0.2 + SG-13.7); no AMM/CPMM/orderbook/MarketOrder/MarketTrade/PriceIndex tokens (FORBIDDEN_LEGACY_TOKENS Layer 2 scan). Production claim: "TB-13 establishes the Polymarket / CTF mathematical core (1 locked Coin = 1 YES_E + 1 NO_E) as a non-trading collateral + share accounting layer. CompleteSetMintTx + CompleteSetRedeemTx + MarketSeedTx are agent-signed Class 3 typed-tx with submit-time admission control + replay-time Gate 4 verification. Resolution authority is canonical task_markets_t.state set by system-emitted FinalizeReward / TaskBankruptcy (CR-13.6: predicate / challenge outcome cannot be overridden by share state). Six-holding CTF preserved bit-equal across all 3 typed-tx. MIN-semantics complete-set-balanced invariant enforced live. Two architect-spec deviations endorsed by clean-room auditor as tighter-than-spec but require architect ratification before TB-14 PriceIndex builds on this substrate." Open follow-ups (carry-forward, NOT ship blockers): architect ratification of u128 ShareAmount + ResolutionRef removal; OBS_TB13_FENCE_MECHANISM_DOOM_LOOP_2026-05-03.md (PARTIAL-MARKER-MULTILINE; AST-aware fence refactor at TB-14+); OBS_RESOLUTIONS_INDEX_TB15_2026-05-03.md (full canonical ResolutionsIndex at TB-15); OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md (additive carve-out for sequencer.rs); OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md (codebase-wide CO P2.x AgentRegistry pass for non-TB-13 agent variants).

tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs Atom6). First Markov capsule emitted (capsule_id b244f16a1f3bd532d041a40fe39b2b7e7cc12fb58e18b61aedd76a8010eeb1b6) with constitution_hash eec695459c71fbef3685583485deb431fe3b561657b2f285b7c5e7e220e59e03; evidence at handover/evidence/tb_15_markov_capsule_2026-05-03/. Production claim: "TB-15 establishes Lamarckian Autopsy + Markov EvidenceCapsule substrate. AgentAutopsyCapsule (per-agent, per-event, AuditOnly) records loss/bankruptcy events derived deterministically from ChainTape evidence — NEVER LLM self-narration. agent_autopsies_t lives sequencer-side (NOT projected to AgentVisibleProjection per CR-15.1 + halt-trigger #1). TypicalErrorBroadcast clustering at N≥3 emits public_summary text + Cids only — NEVER private_detail_cid bytes. MarkovEvidenceCapsule binds constitution_hash + L4 + L4.E + CAS roots + previous capsule + typical_errors + unresolved_obs as next-session bootstrap default; deeper history requires TURINGOS_MARKOV_OVERRIDE=1. CR-15.3/15.4 STRUCTURALLY ENFORCED via writer signature + halt-trigger #3 file-scan. Constitutional alignment: Art.II.1 + Art.III.1 + Art.III.2 + Art.III.3 + Art.III.4 + Art.V.1 + Art.0.2." Open follow-ups (carry-forward NOT ship blockers): multi-site autopsy wire-in (SlashLoss/ChallengeUnsuccessful/VerifierBondLost — when RSP-3.2/RSP-4 ship); L4/L4.E/CAS root chain-readers in Markov generator (currently zero placeholders); CAS-walking dashboard §15 (currently empty autopsy_event_counts); InitAI agent-side honoring of Markov default (P5 v1); OBS_RESOLUTIONS_INDEX_TB15 explicitly deferred per charter §7-G.

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6039:./handover/directives/2026-05-05_TB18_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:19:### A.2 Q1–Q7 verdict ledger
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6057:./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:153:Per CLAUDE.md §3 every non-trivial TB must declare which flowchart gates it touches. A4 touches admission semantics (FC1) and the agent_verifications_t state index (FC2 boot-from-genesis replayability; FC3 capsule derivation discipline). All three FCs verified at HEAD `69910fe` against the 6-cell smoke `stage_b3_smoke_a4_20260510T222030Z`.
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6058:./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:159:- **`verify_peer` dispatch is a separate code path** from `step` / `complete` / `append`. It does NOT call the oracle (no Lean verification) and does NOT call `r2_write_attempt_telemetry` (no AttemptTelemetry CAS write). It only submits a VerifyTx via `bus.submit_typed_tx`. The submitted VerifyTx routes to L4 (accept) or L4.E (reject) per sequencer admission — both are already counted in `l4_work_attempt_count` / `l4e_work_attempt_count` (note: the "work" naming is legacy; the chain accepts both WorkTx and VerifyTx onto the L4 ledger; the FC1 counters cover both per CLAUDE.md §6 OBS_TB18R_INV1_NONLLM_TX clarification).
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6068:./handover/directives/2026-05-10_TB_N1_AGENT_ECONOMY_A4_§8_PACKET.md:203:`FC-trace: FC1-N7 δ Agent externalized output enriched + FC1 hard invariant + FC2 genesis-tape-CAS replay tuple preserved + FC3 capsule-derived-from-tape preserved + Step-2.5 / Step-3 / Step-3.5 / Step-5b admission gates as new predicate-inputs.`

SG-N3.5 + SG-N3.6 directly witness no ghost liquidity. `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` (extended for TB-N* successor markers) keeps the price-as-truth fence active. | ✅ PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6081:./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_N3_§8_PACKET.md:121:[x] No f64 money math                             — adapter helpers use MicroCoin / u128 only
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6084:./handover/directives/2026-05-06_TB18R_PHASE_2_REMEDIATION_DIRECTIVE.md:195:**Sequencer impact (Class-4 admission)**: the sequencer's `From<LeanErrorClass> for RejectionClass` mapping (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs`, R3-shipped) is **not** affected — sequencer only consumes `AttemptOutcome` for the rejection arm via `outcome` discriminator, and `step_partial_ok` does not enter the rejection arm (predicate_passes=false routes to L4.E only when AttemptOutcome ∈ {LeanFail, ParseFail, SorryBlock, LlmErr}). `PartialAccepted` is a **non-rejection, non-acceptance third state** — `step_partial_ok` continues to be CAS-only per R3 §1.3 (no L4 / no L4.E entry). The sequencer mapping function gains a single guard: if `outcome == PartialAccepted` reaches the rejection arm (which it must not), panic in test-build / log-warn-and-fall-back-to-PredicateFailed in release-build.

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6368:./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:85:This confirms FC1-N31..N40 covers the runtime/proposal loop (where partial-verdict arises). FC2 covers the audit layer (where assert_45 lives).
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6371:./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:103:**Implication**: any fix that "improves" assert_45 to admit partial-verdict is treating a symptom on FC2 while the constitutional contract sits on FC1. The contract that says "what shape may a `LeanResult` take?" is an FC1 contract.
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6374:./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:114:The asymmetry matters: FC2 invariants are downstream of FC1 schema. If FC1 schema admits an ambiguous state (e.g., `error_class=None` could mean partial-verdict OR a hypothetical bug), FC2 invariants cannot fully discriminate it.
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6375:./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:116:**Implication**: the right place to typify "partial-verdict is a legitimate third state" is the **FC1 schema**, not the FC2 invariant.
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6383:./handover/directives/FC_FIRST_ANALYSIS_ASSERT45_PARTIAL_VERDICT_2026-05-06.md:251:4. **Allows `verified` to remain the FC2 ship-gate metric** while `verdict_kind` becomes the typed discriminator. Existing `pput_verified` and ChainDerivedRunFacts code paths that read `verified: bool` continue to work without retrofit.

No new replay logic.
handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:6514:./handover/directives/2026-05-11_TB_G_G1_1_§8_PACKET.md:67:| `tests/constitution_g1_resume.rs` (NEW) | 5+ tests covering SG-G1.1..G1.5: <br>• `resume_on_empty_repo_equals_legacy_genesis` — `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME=1` on a fresh empty path produces byte-equal `QState` to legacy `build_chaintape_sequencer` (default branch).<br>• `resume_on_n_entry_chain_sets_next_logical_t_to_n` — bootstrap fresh → commit N entries → drop sequencer → re-`build_chaintape_sequencer` with resume=1 → assert `Sequencer.next_logical_t == N` AND next commit's `parent_state_root` matches.<br>• `resume_balances_reconstruction_matches_forward_replay` — fresh forward run of K accepted `WorkTx` produces `balances_t_A`; same chain replayed via resume produces `balances_t_B`; assert byte-equal.<br>• `non_empty_runtime_repo_only_fires_when_resume_false` — non-empty repo + resume=0 still returns `BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo` (back-compat regression gate).<br>• `pinned_pubkeys_preserved_across_resume` — fresh bootstrap writes `pinned_pubkeys.json`; resume reads it and `epoch` carries forward (no re-pin). |

handover/audits/CODEX_G2_TB_G_G1_1_PRE8_AUDIT_R1.md:9259:255:"src/runtime/chain_derived_run_facts.rs" = "8c6cc83fae725d0390bb079c18294a1be938eca0640302550d6bd20ebf86f8c7"  # rehashed by TB-G G1.1 (2026-05-11 session #40): `fresh_config` test helper extended with `resume_existing_chain: false` field (RuntimeChaintapeConfig schema bump from packet §2). Pure additive default; no production logic touched. Predecessor TB-C0 round-7 hash cdbca2e6 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-C0 round 7 strengthened Bug 2 filter (2026-05-07; per CODEX_TBC0_STRICT_CONSTITUTIONAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_v2_2026-05-07.md Q-RR1 CHALLENGE + §4 condition #1): synthetic L4.E gate filter now binds 5 conditions (agent_id + decoded WorkTx.stake==0 + decoded zero signature + tx_id ends with -atom3-l4e-synthetic-rejection + synthetic_rejection_label.json marker file present and true). Plus cardinality check: if marker present, MUST find exactly 1 synthetic gate or return ChainDerivedError::Cas with anomaly diagnostic. Replaces the round-6 agent_id-only filter that Codex flagged as not-robust. Predecessor round-6 hash a4db21bb superseded.  # rehashed by TB-C0 Codex-§8 Bug 2 fix (2026-05-07; per CODEX_TBC0_STRICT_CONSTITUTIONAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_2026-05-07.md Q5+Q9 remediation): synthetic L4.E gate filter — Work-kind rejections with agent_id == "tb6-smoke-agent" (atom A.1 / TB-6 Atom 3 well-known synthetic-sponsor) excluded from l4e_work_attempt_count. Closes Codex VETO §8 #1 + restores constitutional 3-term invariant accuracy on omega-success runs (P03/P06/P07 expected delta=1 → 0 post-fix). Class 3 fix in chain_derived only — no sequencer.rs touch — confirmed by Codex Q5 PASS clarification that "likely sequencer admission" was speculative; the actual emit-site discriminator (synthetic_rejection_label.json + agent_id pattern) already exists at experiments/minif2f_v4/src/chain_runtime.rs::write_synthetic_l4_l4e_gate_and_genesis_report. Predecessor Bug 3 hash 6ec00047 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-C0 strict-audit Bug 3 fix (2026-05-07; STRICT_AUDIT_TBC0_TAPE_2026-05-07.md Finding C): tail-additive `pub capsule_anchored_attempt_count: u64` (#[serde(default)]; backward-compat); delta now 3-term `l4 + l4e + capsule_anchored - expected` per CLAUDE.md FC1 hard invariant; compute_run_facts_from_chain_with_invariant counts AttemptTelemetry records with outcome == AttemptOutcome::PartialAccepted (variant 6, Phase 2 CAS-only per §3.2 + R3 §1.3). Strictly additive: when capsule_anchored == 0 (pre-fix evidence), formula reduces to original 2-term shape; existing TB-18R R4 invariant tests pass unchanged. Not a typed-tx schema bump — chain_derived_run_facts is a runtime-derived facts struct, not canonical-signing-payload. Predecessor TB-18R R4 hash 6fc9ba7d superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18R R4 (2026-05-06; preflight handover/ai-direct/TB-18R_R4_STEP_B_invariant.md §1.1 + §3): G1-ratified canonical contract — extends ChainDerivedRunFacts with 6 FR-18R.4 v2 fields (expected_completed_attempts / l4_work_attempt_count / l4e_work_attempt_count / attempt_aborted_count / delta / terminal_halt_class), all #[serde(default)]; adds AttemptCountInvariantInputs + AttemptCountInvariantViolation + DrainBarrierViolation types; adds compute_run_facts_from_chain_with_invariant() + attempt_count_invariant() + verify_chain_quiescent_post_drain() ship-gate fns. G1 equation populated WITHOUT alteration per Codex Q1+Q4 remediation: `evaluator_reported_completed_llm_calls == l4_work_attempt_count + l4e_work_attempt_count` (clean halt) + auxiliary `expected + aborted == l4 + l4e` (abort halt). Drain barrier honored via existing ChaintapeBundle::shutdown() — no new Sequencer public method (avoids redundant Class-4 sequencer touch). Predecessor TB-7R hash 87323282 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-7R parent_tx state ruling (2026-05-02): added pub enum ParentTxState (4 variants) + ChainDerivedRunFacts.parent_tx_state field + WorkTxAttempt accumulator + compute_parent_tx_state helper. Per architect verdict 2026-05-02 (parent_tx is conditional invariant, not unconditional smoke requirement). See handover/directives/2026-05-02_TB7R_PARENT_TX_DAG_SMOKE_VERDICT.md + handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TRACE_MATRIX_TB7R_ORPHANS_2026-05-02.md.

## Constitutional alignment note (REVIEW THIS)

The deviation from packet §2's wording is **correct and strictly aligns with the TuringOS v4 Constitution**.

The constitutional anchor for deterministic rebuilds (FC2 §3.2 + §4.1 G-009 Path C) clearly states that the canonical QState replay primitive is `replay_full_transition`. The helper `head_t_witness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs` is explicitly scoped for derived-view boundary construction (Stage A3 SG-A3.4) and does not reconstruct the full core state (e.g., `state_root`, `economic_state_root` must already be provided). Utilizing `replay_full_transition` ensures that `resume` operates as the exact boot-time instance of the canonical replay logic, correctly upholding the architect's mandate to strictly follow the three flowcharts (FC1, FC2, FC3). 

## Q1

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** The `bootstrap_resume_state` helper strictly propagates errors via the `?` operator. It fail-closes if `pinned_pubkeys.json` or `initial_q_state.json` are missing, if they fail to parse, and if `replay_full_transition` yields an error. There is no partial admission pathway.

## Q2

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** The branch uses `std::fs::read_to_string` and `serde_json::from_str` to read and parse `pinned_pubkeys.json` deterministically. No clock, random, or external environmental inputs are introduced into the read path beyond the documented `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME` env-gate.

## Q3

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** `Sequencer::new` is correctly implemented as a thin delegator (`Self::new_at_logical_t(..., 0)`). `Sequencer::new_at_logical_t` houses the core body, ensuring that all admission arms, predicate gates, and monetary invariants are perfectly identical across both construction patterns. 

## Q4

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** The `from_env` implementation uses strict equality via `matches!(std::env::var("TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME").as_deref(), Ok("1"))`. This removes any truthy-string footgun (e.g. `true`, `yes`, `1 `) from triggering the resume branch unexpectedly.

## Q5

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** The `constitution_g1_resume.rs` test suite includes `sg_g1_4_non_empty_runtime_repo_only_fires_when_resume_false`, which specifically asserts that legacy fail-close behavior is preserved. The test suite is correctly registered and run via `scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh`.

## Q6

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** `src/state/sequencer.rs` is securely hashed to `cff248695d1d399c7d6e4cfdce337271d50ea357236d869d732b09ad3cdd301f` and `src/runtime/mod.rs` to `f72bbda70756958c83f5873b3b75585196e7ce821f82e0ca65de32f60f244875`. The system's Trust Root manifest (`genesis_payload.toml`) accurately tracks these rehashes along with proper documentation of the STEP_B atom.

## Q7

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** A full `grep_search` of the touched files confirmed no unexpected f64 arithmetic, shadow ledgers, or global Markov pointers were reintroduced. The `f64` and `shadow` mentions in the files exist purely in docstrings referencing legacy deletions/invariants.

## Q8

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** `resume_existing_chain: false` remains the strict default when `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_RESUME` is not explicitly `"1"`. The SG-G1.4 constitutional test explicitly guards this default-deny posture, proving that legacy TB-N* and Stage C configurations will still throw the `NonEmptyRuntimeRepo` bootstrap error gracefully.

## Q9

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** The `TRACE_FLOWCHART_MATRIX.md` has been successfully updated with the `FC2-INV8` trace row at line 113, structurally tying the TB-G G1.1 release to the `sg_g1_*` test suite and constitutional replay requirements.

## Q10

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** Replay determinism is fully preserved. The `git_writer.read_at(t)` loop reconstructs all `LedgerEntry` objects exactly as they were committed (which naturally includes both accepted L4 and rejected L4.E evidence outcomes). The `replay_full_transition` correctly maps these transitions identically to a real-time run.

## Q11

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** Pinned pubkey continuity is explicitly preserved. The `bootstrap_resume_state` helper reads the existing historical keys into memory, appends the newly generated key, increments the top-level manifest epoch to `max_epoch + 1`, and then flushes back to disk. Test `sg_g1_5_pinned_pubkeys_preserved_across_resume` fully asserts this operational continuity.

## Q12

**Verdict:** PASS
**Conviction:** high
**Justification:** The driver prevents race conditions via the `tokio::select! { biased; }` shutdown loop in `run_chaintape_driver`. Upon receiving the shutdown signal, the channel explicitly closes the queue receiver to reject new envelopes before executing a synchronous, ordered drain of the remaining pending envelopes via `apply_one`.

## Aggregate verdict

Verdict: PASS
Conviction: high
Recommendation: PROCEED

When `cargo test --workspace` re-runs on a newer kernel (different keystore RNG seed → different ed25519 pubkeys, different state-root hashing of new tx fields), it overwrites:
handover/alignment/OBS_EVIDENCE_DRIFT_ROOT_CAUSE_2026-05-07.md:31:| `tb_7_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-01/{replay_report,chain_derived_run_facts,agent_pubkeys,README}.json/md` | `tests/tb_7_atom6_chain_backed_smoke.rs` | 243-254 | `Path::new("handover/evidence/tb_7_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-01")` |
handover/alignment/OBS_EVIDENCE_DRIFT_ROOT_CAUSE_2026-05-07.md:32:| `tb_13_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-03/{replay_report,agent_pubkeys,README}.json/md` | `tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs` | 424-446 | `Path::new("handover/evidence/tb_13_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-03")` |
handover/alignment/OBS_EVIDENCE_DRIFT_ROOT_CAUSE_2026-05-07.md:33:| `tb_14_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-03/{replay_report,agent_pubkeys,pinned_pubkeys,genesis_report,README}.json/md` | `tests/tb_14_chaintape_smoke.rs` | 351-381 | `Path::new("handover/evidence/tb_14_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-03")` |
handover/alignment/OBS_EVIDENCE_DRIFT_ROOT_CAUSE_2026-05-07.md:35:The path is a literal `Path::new(...)` with **no env override, no timestamp suffix, no exists()-check guard**. The comment on tb_7 line 241 even acknowledges "best-effort; if dir unwritable, the test still passes" — i.e., **writes-by-default** semantics. The pubkey diff (different SHA-256 in agent_pubkeys.json) and changed `final_state_root_hex` / `head_commit_oid_hex` are perfectly consistent with re-deriving keystores from a fresh tempdir on the running kernel — keystore generation in cargo tests typically uses `tempfile::TempDir` so each run produces fresh keys, hence different pubkeys → different signatures → different state-root tx-chain.

### 3.2 FC2 — Boot / Genesis Gate

Every real evidence run must be replayable from:

- `genesis_report`
- ChainTape
- CAS
- agent registry
- system pubkeys

Forbidden:

- memory-only preseed

## 17. Reporting Standard

Every run report must include:

- commit HEAD
- binary build identity
- command used
- risk class
- `genesis_report` path
- ChainTape path
- CAS path
- agent registry path
- system pubkeys
- `attempt_count_equality_report`
- replay report
- dashboard regeneration statement

For formal proof benchmark reports, include:

- ΣPPUT
- Mean PPUT on solved
- 95% CI if reporting aggregate
